Business Name: MedVet Silicon Valley
Category: Animal hospital, Emergency veterinarian service, Veterinarian

Real Customer Reviews (Total: 10):
[1] WARNING!!!! NEGLIGENCE!!!! We took our dog for emergency care to Med Vet Silicon Valley located at 7160 Santa Teresa Blvd, San Jose Ca 95139. Our dog had been bitten by another dog.  Before leaving our house we cleaned the deep wound that was actively bleeding, stopped the bleeding and bandaged him properly. We arrived at Med Vet Silicon Valley at approximately 4:50 pm.  We called the  number that we were told to call and filled out the online form. A technician came out and took our dog in.  Our home is about 40 minutes away depending on traffic and our phones were on low percentage with our chargers at home. After approximately 30 minutes of waiting we received a phone call from the veterinarian she explained to us that she wanted to do bloodwork, do xrays, heavily sedate our dog and clean the wound.  We asked her why all this was necessary and she gave us a vague response lecturing us that is needed to be done and that they would call us for an estimate of cost.  We asked more questions and how long it would take and her response was well into the evening and they would call for an estimate.   We waited approximately 20 minutes with no chargers and phones starting to die we went to the door and a technician came out after another 5 minutes we told them our situation and that we only wanted our dog to be treated for the wound. He said he would need to talk to the doctor. After another 10 minutes I called and explained the situation. The technician said someone would call us.  After another ten minutes I went to the door and talked to the technician again and told her the doctor will want to talk to you.  We waited another ten minutes and at that point decided we needed to seek care elsewhere.  We waived down another technician explained we would be seeking care elsewhere. She said that is fine I will be right back.  Another 15 minutes passed and no one came out finally any Tech came to talk to someone else and we told them if we could have our dog. She went back inside. 10 minutes later a tech comes out with our dog and I notice immediately that his bandage has been removed and he is bleeding.  The tech with bright pink hair and a major attitude said that will be 200 dollars. At that point I ask for what my dog is bleeding al ot and had no bandages on them. That is negligence!. She refuses to give us our dog and ask us for a card. I then told her I'm not paying that you didn't do anything my dog is bleeding. The girl with bright pink hair said "whatever" and hands us our dog.  Thankfully we were able to rush our dog to a family friend that lives about ten minutes away and they gave us a first aid kit to stop the bleeding and bandage the wound.  We went to another facility and were able to take our dog take care of. Med Vet 7160 Santa Teresa Blvd, San Jose Ca 95139 showed complete negligence with not bandaging our dog or taking care of them. The other place said there was absolutely no need for heavy sedation or x-rays, treated our dog and we have follow up in a few days. We didn't have time to read the reviews for Med Vet before we went, but now after reading it our experience seems in line with so many other people's experience of negligence and scamming at Med Vet. The pictures show how they let my dog leave and they would not bandage them or even give us back the bandages we had on him.
[2] Positive Experience with Dr. Nielsen. I met with Dr. Nielsen to consult with her on performing surgery (TECA-LBO) for my dog's critical ear problem. She was extremely patient and knowledgeable, and even though ultimately I did not proceed with the surgery at this clinic (mostly because it was slightly more expensive than other places), I felt that that she would have been an excellent choice to perform a surgery.
[3] I had a pet emergency and was told this was the closest place for a walk in. I called to find out the price was minimum 150 and first come basis, so no appointments. I understand that.I took my cat in and was told over the phone what they thought and what I needed to do. (It was about an hour wait. Not bad at all.) They said they would prescribe oral antibiotics and pain medication to take home that lasts 2 weeks so I would need to call to see my primary vet for the rest of the treatment/surgery. I was unhappy with oral medicine since my cat was not eating and I already said she wouldn't let me see her mouth (The issue was dental so that makes sense). They said it was okay to squirt it in the corner of her mouth and should be fine. I insisted on asking for a shot instead and was told I they would have to ask. I got off the phone expecting a call back about that.After hanging up with them, I called my vet to tell them what was going on and how they said I needed dental surgery. My vet was immediately disgusted that the medication was oral too! He said to call them and demand no treatment then bring the cat to him today (despite no appointment and he was close to closing). He said a cat not eating won't and shouldn't have oral medication and I was right to want a shot done.I called MedVet again and they said they had my bill ready and would give me my cat. I was confused since they never contacted me back on treatment so I figured it was just the visit fee.. It was over $240! I asked for a breakdown and they charged the basic 150 with antibiotics and pain medication. They didn't call me back to say what treatment they were doing or anything. They just did what they wanted... Apparently they just gave my cat a shot without talking to me. It was what I asked for but completely rude to not even call me back about it or tell me price too. Certain animals might be allergic to medications! Important to talk to the client...When they were checking me out, I mentioned wanting to know symptoms for the medications given to my cat (Without my actual consent considering they didn't tell me the medications or call me back after I said no to oral.) and was only told drowsiness or dizzy. I asked if I should look out for any bathroom issues like constipation or vomiting. They said no.... I know medications. They gave gabapentin which can do drowsiness and dizziness. However, they gave the antibiotic cefovecin which has side effects of vomiting, diarrhea, lethargy, and decreased appetite. I asked as a test and boy did they fail. Again!Throughout the entire thing, it was like staff didn't talk to each other because they asked the same questions and responded differently when I asked them questions.Me: I have my cats medical history papers with me in the car. I put them in the carrier case. Do you need them? I made copies for you to have.Staff 1: "No we won't need the medical history and bloodwork."Staff 2: "Oh you have that? Yeah. We need those."Me: I brought the tooth that I found so the doctor can see it too.Staff 1: Oh they won't need that.Staff 2: We won't need that. Thanks for bringing it.Staff 2 had to come back out to my car to get the tooth later.....Overall, I was upset and just wanted my cat back. I paid the money and got the hell out of there after checking to make sure I at least got the right pet. They didn't even return the tooth for me to show to my vet... Not going back for it.Tip: You can sort the Google reviews by "Newest" to see that there are a lot of bad reviews in the last year.  Wish I had looked at these prior to coming here.
[4] If I could give negative stars to MedVet Silicon Valley, I would.  Our dog, Max, had a heart condition which unfortunately took a sudden turn for the worst.  We took Max to MedVet as our primary vet didn’t have appointments available.  The Vet was explaining the treatment options to my wife and I over the phone, and we tentatively decided to try to make Max as comfortable as possible and take him home.  We asked the Vet to let us call him back with a final decision so that we could consult with Max’s primary vet.  He then proceeded to lecture us about how much Max would be suffering, something we were acutely aware of and how horrible and cruel of a decision we were making.  He then abruptly hung up on us.  He wouldn’t even get back on the phone after we made our final decision.  Zero compassion at all.  I don’t know the specific Vets name, but he has no business dealing with animals or people.When we finally got Max back, we could see in his eyes that we were making that right decision, something that our primary Vet at Pinnacle confirmed when we took Max there later to be euthanized.  The concern and compassion that our primary Vet at Pinnacle was in stark contrast to the cruel heartless treatment by the vet at MedVet.  My guess is that he was more motivated by the income that admitting Max for two days would have generated than for his actual well-being.  I would never take another animal to MedVet, and I advise you not to as well.****Update**** The Vet’s name is Ryan Wheeler, the same Ryan Wheeler with similar complaints from other reviewers.
[5] Do not risk your animals life bringing them here!!! They clearly forgot about our dog and had us waiting hours and they only took her vitals. It cost $170 for vitals and that is it. We were waiting for blood tests and they forgot!! And just had her and us waiting for nothing!! This place has no idea what they are doing and they are extremely unprofessional.
[6] My dog was post seizure and I was told I needed to wait in my car or I needed to leave by the extremely rude front desk receptionist with brown hair.
[7] It has taken a long time to do this review because of my grief.  I had such a hard time getting over my Mickey.  However i feel it is necessary to say how wonderful this hospital is.  I have had to put pets down before but I have never been treated with such respect and dignity.  I originally came in for a second opinion, as my vet at the time said over the phone well just come in and I will put him down.  Those were her very words !  So I went to Silicon Valley for an opinion and the Dr. who saw Mickey said, we could spend about 4-7 thousand to try but , I really think you have done a good job keeping him alive for as long as you have, it may be time to say good bye.  How eloquently put!  My poor Frenchie was born with a small breathing tube and was on medication his whole life,  he almost made it to ten years old.  I knew that the Dr. was saying it was ok to let him go, and Mickey knew it was time.  We made arrangements to come back toeuthanize, and they took us into a special room and said take your time, when the time came , the Dr. explained everything she was going to do.  I had Mickey on my lap, she gave him the first shot and he kissed her good bye.  Even the Dr. was in tears at this point.  After everything was over. about 3 days later I received a wonderful card that the whole office signed.  I can't tell you how much that meant!!!!  Thank you from the bottom of my heart. I would like to give an update on my new dog.  He had soft palate surgery here and the results are fantastice.  My boy breathes so much better now.  I am really happy that i chose Silicon Valley Vet.
[8] The staff & doctors were very professional and made our visit exceptional!
[9] Our pets, we love them, right? It's the reason as to why all of you are willing to immediately go to an animal emergency care center when you learned that your pet's health is in danger. However, like many other things in life, you will find wretched scums who will try to exploit this innocent love. This place, is filled with these kind of people. I would've remained ignorant of this, if it weren't for the fact that I've experienced it firsthand for myself.I have a cat, who swallowed a 1.5 inch needle. I wasn't certain he swallowed it at the time, so I immediately looked for an emergency care center, and ended up taking him here to get an x-ray. I paid $410 dollars out of my pocket, anxious to know the results. However, I ended up having to spend for an agonizing 2.5 hours, when they originally claimed that they could get it done in 30 minutes. When I asked why it took so long, they claimed that they were "busy," despite the fact that there were only two other patients.The results came in, and it was confirmed: My cat did swallowed a needle. The worry on my face was obvious. One of the staff, seeing this, clearly wanted to exploit this, for he said that to get the needle removed "right now," it would cost more than $9000. Right after he said that, he shrugged, and gave another offer, which is to pay $5500 to get it removed tomorrow morning. If it weren't for the fact that a family member earlier told me that an emergency surgery would've costed approximately $1000 - $2000, I've fallen for this trap. The person showed no compassion as I got my cat back, needle still in his stomach. Before leaving, I asked for a copy of the x-ray image.  They said they will email it to me by 6AM next morning, but I've never received it.  I left the building, and looked back, only to see that same person laughing with the others. I knew then, that I absolutely cannot trust this place.As I got home, I instantaneously looked for another location. That same day, I found a local vet in Evergreen, with a very straightforward doctor who offered that we pay $1500 for x-ray, needle removal, AND medicine, all in one package. I immediately took his offer, and now, my cat is jumping about once again, a needle gone from his stomach.But, it wasn't over yet. Believe or not, this scam disguised as a hospital called me a week later in an attempt to milk even more money out of me, oblivious to the fact that my cat already made the recovery. It wouldn't take a genius to know that I hung up at the spot.
[10] Can't say enough about this organization. We have an Engli$h Bulldog who has had to visit 3 different times for three different random reasons. The expertise, exceptional care  and 24/7 availability is simply the best in the area!!!

User Question: What are the experiences with wait times and communication at MedVet Silicon Valley?

Experiences with wait times and communication at MedVet Silicon Valley vary, with some customers reporting issues. One customer waited approximately 30 minutes after arrival before receiving a phone call from a veterinarian, and then waited additional periods totaling over an hour without clear updates or resolution, leading them to leave for another facility [1]. Another customer experienced a 2.5-hour wait for an x-ray, which was longer than the initially claimed 30 minutes, and noted poor communication about the delay [9]. In another case, a customer waited about an hour, which they considered not bad, but faced communication issues where staff did not call back about treatment options or discuss medication side effects adequately [3]. Additionally, a customer reported waiting hours for blood tests that were forgotten, with no communication about the delay [5]. However, one customer mentioned a positive experience with professional staff and doctors, though specific details on wait times or communication were not provided [8]. Some customers also reported rude behavior from staff, such as a receptionist telling a customer to wait in their car or leave [6], and a veterinarian lecturing and hanging up on customers during a difficult decision [4].

Real Customer Reviews (Total: 10):
[1] Only place open during this quarantine, very good customer service and friendly vibes. Got my 4 watches batteries fixed in less than 10 mins. Thank you !
[2] With all the 5 star reviews I was so happy to find this place nearby! I needed to get a watch engraved and he did it in less than 30 min! I am always in need of a watch repair and change of batteries so I will definitely bookmark this place!
[3] I don't post reviews very often (2nd one on Google), but  I really had to jump in here and write a review for Al.First, a little bit about Al (the proprietor of Time Palace/1st Watch) that I found out by gleaning the internet. Al is a 3rd generation watch maker. He has a son who also works with him in the shop, so that makes 4 generations of watch making in his family. He also has a BS in Electrical Engineering and an MBA. If nothing, at least his career paths indicate a meticulous and dedicated nature.The reason I know all this about him was because I needed to bring in my Rolex for a new crystal, and picking a watch maker to work on your Rolex isn't a casual matter. Finding a watch maker who is familiar with Rolexes is not particularly difficult--even though Rolex makes its own watch movements, the theory behind mechanical watch movements is generally the same across all manufacturers, and Rolex movements (because they make their own) are well discussed and documented. However, because Rolex makes its own watch movements, replacement movements can cost thousands of dollars if your watch maker biffs the innards of your watch. Therefore, finding a good watch maker who is meticulous and stands behind his work is more important than ensuring that your watch goes back to the factory repair center.Onward. Al gave me a straight forward quote to replace my crystal. The price was good to fair. Al also discussed overhauling my watch with me. He indicated that it wasn't necessary (as my watch had just been overhauled last year), but that it was recommended to ensure that the watch continues to function properly.  He left the decision entirely up to me, and his quote for the overhaul was very good. I half expect a watch maker to insist on an overhaul whenever they work on a watch for liability reasons (close up a watch that hasn't had its gasket changed and the watch starts leaking, then suddenly it's the last watch maker's fault). So, I was pleasantly surprised that he didn't insist on an overhaul. I got an overhaul done anyway, but that's beside the point.Al indicated that he would have to order the crystal for my watch. That's fair--at least he does the work himself in-house and doesn't send it off to another watch maker as is the case with many/most jewelry stores who say they work on Rolexes. So, he quoted me "a few days, but no more than a week." If I wanted it sooner, he could arrange for overnight shipping of the new crystal. No thanks, I said--take your time and I'll pick it up "whenever.""Whenever" turned out to be the next day before noon. Whiskey. Tango. Foxtrot. That was fast! I dropped the watch off at 3:30 the day before, and he had a new crystal in his shop, replaced it, and overhauled my watch (which includes taking it apart, cleaning the parts individually, replacing all the gaskets, polishing/buffing fine scratches out of the case and band, and putting it all together), and called me before noon the next day.Bonus: He also puttied off a smudge from the movement of another watch I was wearing when I came in to pick up my Rolex. The fingerprint could be seen through the caseback viewing window and must have been there from when it was assembled. Gratis. On the spot. Couldn't have hoped for more.In sum: 1) Fast. Really fast. 2) Highly professional, educated, and clear communicating proprietor. 3) Work done on-site in a very clean and well organized work shop. 4) Skilled enough to not be afraid of people watching over his shoulder while he fiddles with microscopic watch parts under a microscope.
[4] Very nice service!!! Fast and good quality of work!!!They repair my jewelry the same day, it’s amazing!!!
[5] The pace at which the job is done and the quality is fantastic
[6] Al does excellent work! I first came to Time Palace Jewelers to simply have a watch battery replaced. Al did it on the spot and charged a fair price. After having a few more batteries replaced, I brought in a Bulova watch to have the movement replaced. I think Al said it would take about three days to order and replace the movement, but the next afternoon he called to tell me my watch was ready. Today I picked up my 44 year old mantle clock I bought in Sweden in 1977. I dropped it off on Saturday; four business days ago. The clock looks great and runs perfectly! I highly recommend Al and Time Palace Jewelers!
[7] I needed a battery and a crown (the turn-y thing). Total $20.00. The watch fixer person was friendly and answered all my questions w a smile and snicker... Like "what's your favorite watch." He reminded my of Geppetto... except he doesn't make puppets (nice spirited). Although that's an assumption I didn't ask him that. He sat next to me while he worked on my watch. He was amused that I've need wearing a broken watch for quite some time. (who checks thier watch for time?). As I left he thanked me for coming in and said "now you can control your watch." :-)
[8] When I was younger, I took my engagement ring and wedding band to a local jeweler to get polished.  When they told me that my setting needed to be reinforced or I'd lose my diamond, I was scared enough to go ahead and have them do it without getting a second opinion/quote.  They did the work for $50, and boy did I ever get what I paid for; it was uneven, and actually had a noticeable bubble in it.  The ring looked bad, the tension setting wasn't sleek and flush to my diamond like it used to be... but I was young and didn't want to fight, nor did I have a ton of money to find someone skilled enough to fix it properly.Fast forward eleven years and a lot of damage to the ring.  There were big nicks and cracks in my ring and I wanted it to get remade so that it looked as beautiful as it was when my husband proposed. I spent a lot of time reading reviews before deciding to head over to Time Palace. What made me decide to trust Internet-Al were his thoughtful, firm responses to negative reviews.  He reminded me of my father, an experienced, honest craftsman who is never afraid to tell someone, "I won't do it for less than $X because if I tried, the work would be bad, and my reputation means a lot to me. I won't do shoddy work to meet your price point."So I dropped the ring off two days ago along with  a picture of what I hoped Al could do to it.  He gave me a price of between $200-400, depending on labor hours.  I told my husband it would be closer to $400 since I believe in underpromising and overdelivering.I picked up my ring today and I am thrilled: it is stunning again: sleek, modern, and flawless. To top it all off, Al only charged $200, the very bottom end of what I expected to pay.If you have a piece that means a lot to you, and you want someone to take good care of it, someone who stakes his reputation on doing his best work every time, head over to Time Palace and entrust it to Al.  You won't be disappointed.
[9] I have been driving past here since I lived to the neighborhood and I just stopped in to have the links on my watch repaired. I would recommend this place to anyone who wears a watch they are quick, professional and very friendly. Not to mention it is very reasonably priced.
[10] My ring finger apparently shrunk between when we sized it and when my now-fiance bought the ring. We came in close to closing (because that was the only time we could go) and we didn't feel rushed at all with the process. I was kind of at a 1/4 size when using the ring sizing tool, but he explained that because there is open space from the bridge on the underside of my ring that it would feel slightly looser than the sizing ring.My ring is silver and they explained that it would be less expensive to do a gold solder, but that there would be a tiny gold spot at the base of the band. If we used silver solder, there was a risk that the garnet gem would crack from the increased heat, so he would have to remove and reset the gem to avoid that. With the extra cost to remove/reset the gem after soldering it, it was $75. Slightly higher than we expected based on the reviews, but it was less than what my sister paid for hers at South Coast Plaza, and she didn't even need the gem reset because her ring was gold.The ring was ready in 3 business days - 2 days earlier than I needed it - and the ring was even shinier than when my fiance proposed.When I got the ring back, it did fit better than the sizer ring because of the open space.My only complaint is that there was a teeny tiny pockmark where the solder is. I don't wear the ring that often because I work with dogs (I mostly wear it on a velvet cord around my neck), and I actually prefer my silver tarnished (blaspemous, I know) so it won't show and it wasn't a huge deal for me. However, I could see how that could be a big deal for someone else (although I have no idea how common that is for that to happen). So I guess I would deduct 1/2 star for that.Overall I would definitely recommend them.

Time Palace Jewelers offers jewelry repair services, including polishing, remaking, and resizing rings, as well as repairing watch links and other jewelry items [4][8][9][10]. For example, one customer had a ring remade and picked up within two days, while another had a watch battery replaced in less than 10 minutes [1][8]. [7] TLDR: Doctor Melissa Roth and Katrina and all the nurses at ACCESS saved my dog's life, bringing her back from a 105 multiday fever, not eating or drinking, shaking when I tried to touch her, urinating on herself, and an incredible fast spreading skin infection combo. Because of COVID they have you park and wait in your car and a nurse will come and bring your animal inside the hospital, and call you with any updates, just FYI while COVID is still applicable.Full Story:My 8 year old german shepherd had a 105 fever and was lethargic, not eating or drinking, her normal vet is VCA Hermosa and it was after hours, and they recommended we go to ACCESS Animal Hospital. I'd never taken my dog to an animal hospital before so I wasn't sure what to expect.I called ACCESS and they were so nice on the phone. Talking to them made me feel at ease. I took my dog in at midnight on Sunday and they checked her out, gave her fluids under the skin and took an xray of her lungs and abdomen to see if they could find anything. Thankfully her x-rays were clean. They gave her an anti-nausea shot to see if she would eat and drink after it. We originally thought she just ate some bad food and was having an elevated temperature as it work through her systemWe took her home and then checked her out again in the morning. She still had the fever, but she was at least drinking, unlike the night before. So thinking it was no longer fever due to food, and that it wasn't going away by itself,We decided to take her back to ACCESS and we met the wonderful Dr. Roth. She really took care of my dog, and was in constant contact with us about her recommendations and care opinions for my girl.My dog's fever case was a bit out of the normal, the basic tests for infection kept coming back as normal, yet she still had a 105 fever, and then she got a rapidly spreading skin infection. It was weird, so we opted for more tests.With the help of Dr. Roth we ended up doing a blood test, a urine test, a tick test, a blood clotting test and finally an ultrasound to check for an infection, a UTI, tick diseases, arthritic joint infection and any infections localized in her smaller organs.These tests were not all at once, we did one at a time to see if we could find anything, we only did the next test after the results came back negative. They were not pushy about tests at all. After every test, Dr. Roth would call us and explain the pros and cons of doing each and every test, and she gave us her opinion on whether or not she would do each test for her own sick pet if she was in our situation. She also called us after every result came in. We ended up talking to her a lot.Dr. Roth and her medical assistant Katrina were incredibly warm, comforting, knowledgeable and empathetic. Dr. Roth educated us on all of our opitions and all of the tests we could do at ACCESS to try and figure out what was wrong with our girl. Katrina was my email point of contact relaying what Dr. Roth said about my dog when they looked at pictures I sent her during her recovery.We eventually left with an antibiotic for the fever, an antibiotic for her skin infection, and an anti-tick medication. My puppy got immediately better and had been better ever since going to ACCESS. I had been in email communication with Katrina and Dr. Roth to monitor her recovery and they were so responsive and so excited that Jordan was getting better.It was so comforting knowing that they cared so much about my dog. They helped make a very scary week for my family so much better and I have my dog back now thanks to them.
[8] This place exceeded my expectations. Everyone there was kind and respectful. I took my dog in for an emergency surgery and he is now home safe and sound. The Dr came in to talk to me before I went home and she called me before and after the surgery as well as the next morning before I picked him up and kept me informed the whole time. I could not have asked for better treatment.Thank you Access!!!! ❤️
[9] There are very nice when we came in for emergency visits. So our pups were exposed to a parvo virus and we put 3 pups in there one of them is doing great and the 2 are working there way there but i started to get worried because we read the reviews after we put our pups in there but im pretty sure ACCESS has it all covered because they are treating our pups well...Hopefully they survive the virus....
[10] My puppy had an emergency and I was treated with empathy the whole time. From the front desk, to the veterinarians, everyone was perfect. The cost was also reasonable. Thank you so much, I’m very grateful.

[1] technicians were good, but two phone calls I made to a technician to ask a question two months ago have not been answered.I will ask for them to stop service and refund what I have paid.

[3] The technician for our routine scheduled treatment was prompt and efficient!  Although bee season had not started yet, he advised us to call for service as soon as nests started to appear in our eaves even if it is before or next scheduled service date.  It is refreshing that a service like Terminix makes you feel like you are important!
[4] I have the yearly inspection insurance for dry and sub termites. Been using this service for years and have the same inspector each year. Used Covid precautions this year for inspection. Always courteous.
[5] We have had Mario Macias as are rep for several months now and he is informative and always goes out of his way to make sure we are happy and bug free.  Thank you,  06-10-19
[6] Bate and Switch at its finest! I have been a customer of this company for years. When Antimite become Terminix things went crazy. We have always requested to know when they are coming to make sure our pets are secured. Then for the last year every time they come the bill comes it is more than what we had agreed upon. Back in January, they say they came and treated the home. Yet the day before when the tech called I advised him that would not work and not to come that day. A few days later I receive a bill for $109.00. We were never advised that they were going to more than double the cost of the service that we had with them and we were also paying for a treatment when we had requested them to not come. ON 1/31 I spoke with Victor who is a manager and he stated that he was going to take care of the issues. He would credit our account for $60 $30 at one time and then another $30 a few days later and then what our monthly bill would be back to the amount that we had agreed to pay moving forward. I have the tech home out on 2/7 and I paid the tech right then and there as I have always done. by darn it the bill comes this week showing no credit for my check and show right at $25 more than what we agreed. You try to reach them and it is just a mess. Just hate being advised that someone is going to do something and then they don't and you try to reach them there is no way to do. The CS rep can't give me Victor's number unless he agrees to have me have. This is just crazy.
[7] I'm trying to cancel all 3 services I have with Terminix due ONLY to their inept customer service.  The technicians I've had the pleasure to work with have been fabulous - courteous, professional, responsive and very effective.  They go the extra mile when asked for help. I've been with Terminix for something like 15 years at 2 different addresses in different parts of the US.  If it was just on the strength of the technicians I would recommend Terminix unreservedly with 5 stars.That's why cancelling my services is so painful, because I know there are hard-working, dedicated professionals who work there.Unfortunately the customer service or account administration is woeful.  This includes billing and just getting to a person that can help (that is not a technician).  As I write this, I have been on hold for 35 minutes trying to reach someone in the "cancellation department" to cancel my services.Here follows a more detailed description of the sad state of Terminix's customer support in my case.For the 1st 2 months of 2021, I got no invoices from Terminix for my termite protection services.  I called Terminix in January to try and understand what was going on, but all the people I spoke to could not understand what was going on either.  Call-backs that were promised never happened.  Finally in March I got 3 months worth of invoices in 1 invoice that simply said I was past due.  No explanation or apology, and I was made to feel responsible for what turned out to be an  invoicing issue at Terminix that affected a lot of customers.My next invoices in April had a 6% price increase across the board.  No explanation was given and no warning.  I talked to several phone reps about the increase.  They all promised call backs from the "local" office that never materialised.  The last rep I spoke to said that it was an oversight and that the original charge amount was correct and would be reinstated.  Again, he said he was filing this with the local office who would contact me. Nothing happened, and the charges stayed the same.When I called back, a different rep sent an email to the local branch service manager to ask for follow-up.  (I know this because I saw a copy of her email).  The local branch service manager then sent me an email with the data he had scraped from Zillow about my house and said that since my house was worth so much money that paying Terminix seemed "pretty low for a house of that value".Caifornia is not known for cheap real estate, so I pointed out to him that the cost to rebuild the house is a fraction of the "value" he had pulled off Zillow.  Most of the value is in the land.This same person then took a different tack and told me by email that they had to increase rates due to increases in cost.  When I asked why there was no notice and why this increase was not aligned with the contract renewal (as required in my contract), I got radio silence.After about a week, I called again, and this time was called back by another "local" rep.  I couldn't take the call and it went to voice mail where he left me a call-back number.  I called that number within a few hours and left him a voice mail message inviting him to call me.  Now several days later, there has been no response.So here I am, very frustrated by the ineffectiveness of my efforts to understand (and help) Terminix's billing and customer service process, and unwilling to be disrespected by their "local" customer service any more.  I will be sending my technician an email saying how sorry I am to be parting company and thank him for everything he has done for me.I hope if you have made it this far, you have found this review helpful.PS It took 50 minutes to connect with someone who could help me cancel my contracts.  She was super friendly and very apologetic, and is a great example of what good customer service can be.  Terminix could use more people like her.
[8] We have had great service with Terminix.  Wayne our technician always calls the day before we are scheduled to be serviced and he arrives within the time slot we were given.  He checks to see if we are experiencing any new problems or need any added areas treated. We would recommend Terminix  for their service as well.as their very reasonable prices.
[9] My score isn't related to associate/service, but the approach. When I first met with a sales associate and they inspected my home back in 2010 they didn't find any live termites, but said I had termites because they found wood damage and termite droppings even though another company a year prior when I bought the home gave termite clearance report. The past several years during inspections and when I've been doing work to the house I continue to find wood damage, further deterioration, and termite pellets. Now that I have paid double what most companies charge to have my house tented and my annual $250 policy renewal they say that I don't have termites no matter what they see/find and indicate basically I would have to start chopping apart wood to try find live termites. This methodology is not consistent and deceptive. If a house doesn't have termites unless they find live bugs, that should be the litmus test on all inspections including before signing sales contract or buying/selling a home. Now due to all the termite damage most of my decks have progressed with wood rot. So basically I feel that the warranty and protection plan doesn't really do much and is not consistent. This is a systemic company wide issue as this methodology has been projected by every tech except the original tech who sold us on tenting the house and termite protection plan.
[10] Great customer service, reliable, arrived on time, honest estimates, and fair prices.

User Question: What are the main strengths and weaknesses of Terminix's pest control services based on customer experiences?

Based on customer reviews, Terminix's main strengths include professional and effective technicians, with specific technicians like Gustavo, Mario Macias, and Wayne being praised for their personable, informative, and prompt service, including calling ahead and addressing concerns [2][3][5][8]. However, the main weaknesses involve significant issues with customer service, billing, and communication, such as unreturned phone calls, unexpected price increases, billing errors, and difficulties reaching representatives or managers, leading to frustrations and service cancellations [1][2][6][7][9]. [5] Thank you for being inconsiderate and no it WAS NOT THE FLU IT WAS A PARASITE IN MY STOMACH and food poisoning. A stomach bug you get from contaminated meat When people poop and don’t wash their hands. I was definitely sick for two weeks after eating here and have been fine ever since I stopped coming here.This place is obvious CARELESS of their customers and after placing my review and to see your managers reply back and speak recklessly to me saying I had the flu when I have doctor papers to prove otherwise is VERY unprofessional. Now I see why the bunch of poor reviews on this establishment 😂😂  the workers are rude and telling by the Replies the manager is obviously just as rude too .. let’s see how long this place last ...Have a good day!
[6] Better than some but not my favorite.  Portions were good size and price was good.
[7] Los Arroyos offers a good selection of cuisine, including both traditional items, as well as more typical "Mexican food" dining experience. The dining room is small, has ample natural light, and comfortable seating.  Service is fast-casual, with counter ordering and table delivery.I have enjoyed several of their dishes, including the burritos, chimichangas,  and several dinner "specialties." Their molcajete is quite good. Food is neither bland nor too spicy, and the flavor strikes a good medium to appeal to a broad plate.As a local, this is one of the few establishments I frequent in the outlet mall area, as I typically try to avoid crowds and traffic.
[8] First of all, the food here is excellent. It is without question the best Mexican food in Camarillo (don't let it go to your head, that's like the best pork chop in Riyadh- Camarillo is a restaurant wasteland). The ingredients are all high quality and the taste is outstanding. I rate it higher than the Montecito location.Second, this is not a place that caters to locals. It's in the Outlet Mall and likely does not see its customers again.Which is why I gave them 3 stars and not the five they should get. The food is very over priced. I'm always willing to pay more for good food but $14. for nachos is over the top. I've been going since they opened and the plate of nachos I got today was exactly half of their normal portion. My go to dish at Los Arroyos is the molcajete which is always terrific. Today it was 2/3 the size and they eliminated the shrimp. They've stopped giving chips with their entrees now as well.I know their target customer is the one time tourist. As much as I enjoy the food, I can't justify small portions and high prices just because they're catering to the tourist/shopper trade and not the loyalty of locals.
[9] I’m not sure which is better the food or the service. The food is consistently delicious. Wednesday and Friday are tamale days and we look forward to them every time, fantastic!!! The service is friendly and efficient. Once you’ve been there, you are remembered each time you come back. Andrea and Andrea treat us like family, I really can’t say enough good things!!!!!
[10] Why have I never came here before! I've been wasting my time across the street at Chipotle, missing out on this goodness. Ever since we walked in, the cashier was super friendly and patient when we ordered. The chips came out nice and warm, the green salsa from the salsa bar was delicious and the cabbage was yummy. My daughter had to go back to refill our little cabbage bowl a couple times, and the food was great. She got the kids taco and it was crazy filled with steak! My green enchiladas were tasty and the black beans were very delicious. All in all, I was very impressed with this place. I have no complaints... well we did wait for about 15 minutes, but it was definitely worth it. Everything tasted fresh, so I didn't mind the wait too much. Please give this place a try, we're definitely coming back soon.

User Question: What are the standout dishes or menu items at Los Arroyos Camarillo Mexican Restaurant based on customer reviews?

Based on customer reviews, standout dishes at Los Arroyos Camarillo Mexican Restaurant include the carnitas, which were described as delicious [3], and the molcajete, noted as quite good and always terrific [7][8]. Other praised items are burritos, chimichangas, and dinner specialties [7], with tamales highlighted as fantastic on Wednesdays and Fridays [9]. The green enchiladas and black beans were also mentioned as tasty and delicious [10]. However, some customers found the nachos overpriced and noted reduced portions [8].

Business Name: Palace Hotel, a Luxury Collection Hotel, San Francisco
Category: Hotel, Meeting planning service, Wedding venue
Description: Elegant rooms & suites in a lavish 19th-century hotel, plus posh dining, a gym & an indoor pool.

Real Customer Reviews (Total: 10):
[1] Dieses Hotel ist jeden Cent wert. Das Personal ist sehr freundlich, die Zimmer sind sehr sauber, das Bett ist überaus bequem und die Lage ist aufgrund der Nähe zu diversen öffentlichen Verkehrsmitteln perfekt für alle Aktivitäten rund um San Francisco. Morgens bietet das Hotel einen kostenlosen Kaffee- und Teeservice, den man sich in der Lobby zubereiten kann. Das interkontinetale Frühstücksbuffet im Restaurant ist das bisher beste, leckerste und variierendste Frühstücksangebot, das ich in den USA gegessen habe. Sehr zu empfehlen und sein Geld wert. Als exklusiven Bonus hatte ich mit meiner Buchung auch ein Upgrade für kostenfreies WLAN im Zimmer. Ein perfekter Städtetrip.
[2] Fantastic warm vibe as you enter the lobby to be greeted by the friendliest staff.
[3] Beautiful modern hotel. Bar/restaurant was a great location for events and happy hour!
[4] Extremely impressive hotel. A visual masterpiece with large conference rooms.
[5] This hotel has a few advantages: the architecture (especially the Garden Court) is superb, the breakfast buffet is large and good, the swimming pool is also very large (especially for a city hotel pool), and it is very well located right on Market street.I have stayed here by myself and with family many times in the past and was always satisfied with the service. However, this year service has been a catastrophe. I wonder if there has been a change in the upper management of the hotel that could explain that; if this is the case, I'd advise Starwood to let the current manager go...First, let's talk about rooms and upgrades. As my booking was done through Amex platinum travel, and as a Starwood platinum member, there were 2 reasons to try and get me the room upgrade. While it is based on availability, the Palace has many rooms, and the chance that all rooms corresponding to an upgrade are full is very slim. I traveled on labor day last year and the year before and the Palace was always able to offer me that upgrade, but somehow this year there was no upgrade available at check-in. The young lady at the front desk (it looks like front desk employees have all been changed) told me that there would be something for the following nights if I checked on the following day. I did that... A young guy checked on his computer, and then had to go and see the manager to tell me there was no upgrade available. Either there was something and he should have given it to me, or there was not and he should have said it right away, but why this need to ask the manager?? I suppose the new manager does not like giving the upgrades, and asks all his front desk staff to always check with him so he can deny what customers should have had.Because we were given a handicap accessible room at check-in, it had a horrible shower that was level with the rest of the bathroom (needless to say, everyone can go into the shower with their dirty shoes..). After being denied the upgrade, we just asked if it was possible to switch rooms (for the same category) to a non-handicap room. The guy checked and saw some free rooms of that category. We did not want to end up in something much smaller, so we asked to see what they had before we could decide to switch or not. Once again, the guy went to see the manager, who didn't want that either.. we would have had to switch to the other room blindly, with the possibility to get into a smaller room!!! In all my hotel stays, I have NEVER seen a manager refuse someone to have a quick look at a room before switching.If the Palace manager wants to save money by not giving upgrades that people should have (and instead leave the room empty in case someone decided to suddenly check-in..), they should realize that the faithful customers who won't bring their business here again will cause a much bigger loss of money... If the manager also does not like giving upgrades, he/she should make sure to drop out of the Amex Fine Hotels & Resorts program...Second, breakfast service. While breakfast is good, many people (like my family and I) have coupons for free breakfast (with Starwood or Amex) and that changes the quality of service... Before we were even served coffee, the waiter (whoever that was, it was the same problem every day) asked if we were to bill it to the room or pay with coupons. Service (tip) is included with the coupons, and needless to say the waiters know that and if they know you have coupons, they won't go out of their way to help you. Why would they do it, when they can pamper people who will give them a big tip, and they know that however poor their service is, they will get the same amount of money from you?.. It was very unpleasant to have the waiters come back regularly during the breakfast to ask about the payment method, while family members were waiting forever to have their coffee cup replenished....Third, room service. This is the first hotel were I was told that emptying the minibar (so we could put some water in it) would cost us $25!!! Other places send someone (to whom you'll give some tip) for free... Then the cleaning of the room was not great. Some papers (used medical pill wrapping) that any good maid service would have trashed were left on my bedside table day after day..We never heard the vacuum cleaner while walking in the corridors while the maids were taking care of other rooms (with the door open).Fourth, valet parking. It felt like here again, the hotel had fired half the staff compared to the previous years. It took forever to retrieve the car each time we wanted to go out, because valet staff was very much reduced to one or two people.The garage is just opposite the hotel in a small alley, so it's not a distance problem... This is very frustrating when you'd like to continue visiting the many places this city offers to a tourist!Fifth, the renovation. While I liked the very classic style before the renovation, I don't mind the new modern style of the rooms. However, the bathroom is worse than before, with almost no place to put toiletries around the sink. One improvement though is the high faucet which allows one to wash one's hands without bumping into the sink as was the case before renovation.With all these issues, the Palace managed to lose a customer who used to come here year after year. I'll miss the Garden Court, and hope that Starwood will quickly realize that if they don't change the way the hotel is currently managed, it will soon run into trouble...
[6] Well done. I live in the city and stay here from time to time. Great staff and relaxing pool.
[7] I spent 25 minutes when booking our reservation with a Starwood representative to get the rooms that we needed for our brief stay to be placed on the same floor....one with a king/queen the other needed two doubles. In order to achieve this I was told by a Starwood representative that my husband and I needed to book 2 grand deluxe rooms which was the only way that we could insure getting the rooms near each other on the same floor. It was important to us that are two rooms were on the same floor as we were traveling with our children. In fact my husband and I chose a smaller room then we initially desired for ourselves in order to accommodate the availability of what was on hand. When hanging up with Starwood I was insured by our representative,who had coordinated directly with the hotel reservations staff, that we would have two grand deluxe rooms with the appropriate bed requirements on the top floor of the hotel that were near each other. That was our expectation when we checked in. Simple enough.We checked into the hotel around 6:30 at night only to find that nothing about the reservation was as I had booked. Once placed in our rooms(Which were on an entirely different floors , each with king beds), we had to move within moments due to the screaming children that were dismantling the room next to us. The hallway leading to the end of the Corredor in which our room was located was littered with cribs, dirty reminants from room service carts and sounded and looked more like a Chucky cheese than it did a luxury hotel. Our second room which was also book-ended by extremely loud people did not have the two double beds that we booked. Both of our rooms were entirely unacceptable and would have made it impossible to have a second's peace for the 30 hours we were to be in San Francisco. The moment when we alerted the front desk of the conditions of the rooms and the error of the booking I was told that my reservations were merely "requests" and that the hotel could only do their best to accommodate those "requests" not guarantee them. From the time we checked in we then waited approximately two hours to be put into two new rooms that were in fact on the same floor though one was a standard room and quite a bit smaller than the room that we booked only to be told that it was a "grand deluxe" room "class". Being beyond reason at this point I found myself arguing the lack of space, additional windows and bathroom size, to prove my point. The other room was a handicap room with a bench in the shower, etc....needless to say, these were not the rooms we booked nor the conditions we expected from a luxury hotel. I was a little more than peeved at the runaround and lack of continuity in the hotel staff's addressing of this issue. At no point in time did a manager apologize for the inconvenience and tell us to go sit in the bar, have a drink on them while they sorted out the rooms and had our bags delivered to the new accommodations . At no point in time was there a note apologizing for leaving us walking about the hotel for two hours at 7 o'clock at night. After an 8 hour drive through a rainstorm from LosAngeles Unfortunately we missed our dinner reservation in San Francisco for one of our nights there and needed to go down to the restaurant at the hotel where we dropped $300 on the dinner bill just to grab a bite while we waited to land somewhere in the hotel.. Some of the front desk people dealing with the reservations were kind others were snooty(only one in particular). I have never seen a hotel that is part of the luxury hotel brand have such an inconsistent staff. At no point in time did the manager intervene. The hotel botched our stay and we felt as though we were the complaining high-need patrons. Never was there an ,"I'm sorry". All we kept being told was, "I see"....code for ,"you're a bother and I don't care".The hotel does have an impressive lobby and lower floor. The rooms do have lovely beds with wonderful sheets. The ceilings are high and if we had wanted a standard room it was quite nice as far as standard rooms go. I really would've been quite happy with hotel had there not been the complete inconsistency in staff and had our accommodations not been so botched. It doesn't matter how magnificent a hotel is if the service is not there. We've stayed at many luxury hotel brand hotels over the years and have never been disappointed until now. The entire point of booking such a hotel is to have the service that one grows to expect. In my opinion the hotel should have comped our stay or made some gesture towards trying to make up for basically what was a lost evening on our first night in San Francisco and was a terrible way to celebrate ours son's 16th birthday evening. Seeing as we were only there for two nights, that was Half of our stay. Our expectations had been to drive in, check-in, freshen up, have cocktails and enjoy a lovely evening out in the city with our children....not babysit a room change with no place to get ready for the night out.As a side-note, we were told that the hotel will be renovating and be done sometime in the spring. Keep that in mind when booking for this hotel as I'm sure that it will be quite loud on the floors near the construction.
[8] We came to the Bay Area two weeks ago to get married in Muir Woods. Even though the ceremony was in Marin and weekend activities were all in Napa and Half Moon Bay, we chose to splurge and spend all four nights at the beautiful Palace hotel in San Francisco, excited about the old world elegance, grandeur, and world-class service. It was out of our way, but we didn't care. I indicated when I made the reservation two months prior that it was our wedding, and even took the time to e-mail the hotel GM prior to our arrival (he never responded). Upon arrival, we were told that there were no upgrades available. I found this odd seeing as I had informed them 2 months ago that it was a once-in-a-lifetime occasion, and had also taken the time to e-mail the GM directly. I figured no worries, they probably had plenty of full-rate customers paying for those rooms. We took the room we were given and were surprised to see a beautiful bouquet of flowers waiting for us. I was heartened and touched at what I thought was a lovely consolation gesture for not being able to upgrade us. I was mistaken - it was a lovely gesture from personal friends of ours, not the hotel.Our room on the 8th floor was elegantly decorated (old-world, but not outdated) and clean. The view was nothing to talk about - looking directly into the windows of adjacent office buildings. The bed was extremely comfortable, and the pillows fluffy almost to the point of discomfort. There was no coffee maker in the room, but happily the fridge was one of those that actually had tons of empty space for guests' items, not just minibar stock. The coffee at the in-house Ghirardelli cafe was to die for anyway. Water pressure in the shower was fantastic. I agree with the other reviewer that noted the lack of available electrical outlets in the room. Also, as another reviewer stated, the walls are VERY thin. I was able to hear the telephone conversation of the guest in the next room quite clearly, as well as numerous guests in the hallways at 2am. As there seem to be a lot of families staying there, the pool at 9pm on a Friday looked like a kids' water park in the summertime. The hot tub was filled with teenagers. We promptly walked right back out and the spa attendant gave us a sympathetic look and said, We open in the morning at 5am! :) All staff we encountered were polite, but not particularly gracious.Hotel location was fantastic, we were able to walk to the mall, the Embarcadero, etc. Cabs were plentiful and easy to hail.I must say that every step of our journey from our home state of FL, people went out of their way to acknowledge our occasion and make it special for us (complimentary champagne on the flight, complimentary champagne at dinner, personalized menus, an upgraded rental car, etc - all without us asking). However, not once did the Palace acknowledge our occasion or do anything to make it special for us. I guess I had wrongly assumed that it was things like this where hotels such as the Palace shine - where they separate themselves from their more standard competitors. We were looking for it to be a special stay at an old-world-elegance hotel. Instead It was a very ordinary stay at old-world-elegance hotel. In fact, by the time Sunday morning had rolled around, we decided not to give the Palace Hotel any more of our money and cancelled the Garden Court brunch reservation for four.I'm giving an overall rating of Average because it was exactly that. There was NOTHING special about this stay at this hotel, and I'm quite disappointed in having personally reached out to the general manager and then receiving not even so much as an acknowledge or reply. For the amount of money that we spent to stay here, we quite frankly expected more.
[9] Muy temprano, al llegar al hotel, nuestra habitación todavía no estaba disponible, por lo que el el personal muy amablemente nos ofreció pagar el Upgrade a una habitación más grande. El pago valió la pena, ya que la habitación era mucha más grande, y hasta nos pusieron una cama extra, ya que éramos 4 personas.Muchas gracias!!
[10] Up until today, I've always had a great experience at The Palace in San Francisco. Today though, I called to request a credit card authorization form, as I have booked a room here for a co-worker, but needed to put the expense on my credit card. After speaking with two individuals at the hotel, I was told emphatically that the policy was that you must request a credit card authorization 72 hours in advance. I was told over and over that this was a policy of the hotel, with no real reasoning behind it. As a Platinum Elite Marriott Bonvoy member, I would highly recommend that the Marriott team review their policies and procedures, and take into account how these policies affect the business traveler staying at their hotels. As someone who books hotels last minute for work, 99% of the time, sending a credit card authorization form 72 hours in advance is not even a possibility. Now, I'm forced to make the choice if I can even keep this reservation, or go to another hotel that can accommodate my needs.

User Question: What are some of the amenities and services mentioned by guests at the Palace Hotel in San Francisco?

Guests have mentioned several amenities and services at the Palace Hotel in San Francisco. The hotel offers elegant rooms and suites with comfortable beds and high ceilings [1][7]. Amenities include a gym and an indoor pool, which is described as large for a city hotel [1][5]. The hotel provides a free coffee and tea service in the lobby in the mornings [1]. Dining options include a bar/restaurant that is noted as a great location for events and happy hour [3], and an intercontinental breakfast buffet in the restaurant that is highly praised for its variety and quality [1][5]. The hotel also has large conference rooms [4] and a beautiful lobby [2][7]. Some guests have reported issues with services such as room upgrades, breakfast service, room service, valet parking, and renovations, but these are specific to certain stays [5][6][7][8][10]. The location is highlighted as convenient due to proximity to public transportation and attractions like Market Street, the mall, and the Embarcadero [1][5][8].

Category: Doctor

Real Customer Reviews (Total: 10):
[1] My experience at this office was amazing. The surgery I underwent was wisdom teeth extraction. They were very careful, friendly and informative before and after the surgery. It was the best oral institution I have ever been to. Nothing less than 5 stars for Dr. Lee :)
[2] I had a great time getting wisdom teeth extractions.The very nice staff, extremely clean and professional office helped to make it not as bad as most people think. I will definitely be coming back
[3] MY ADVICE IS SIMPLY AVOID GOING TO JEFFREY LEE AT ALL COST! I was referred to Lee's office by my general dentist for a biopsy and as a result, the Mental nerve was permanently damaged. I lost sensation of the lower right side of my face; I lost sensation of my teeth and have the feeling of numbness in my lips 24 hours/day. After the biopsy, he decided to refer me to an endodontist to perform a root canal treatment on two teeth. This endodontist made the ice test and confirmed that the nerve was severed and refused to do the root canal. I don't blame him; he did not want to be a part of this massacre. I later visited three other oral surgeons (maxillofacial) and they all were completely surprised and confirmed that damaging the nerve could have been absolutely avoided if the surgeon was skilled. All three surgeons also unanimously said that the root canal for the two teeth was not the correct treatment plan.In summary: *You need to know that Jeffrey Lee is so arrogant and does not listen to you. *He never explained the biopsy process and never told me the consequences of the biopsy and the possibility of damaging the nerve. *While sitting in the dentist chair a few minutes before injecting the anesthesia, one of his team members will come over rushing you to sign a bunch of papers to take your legal right away. Do not sign any of these papers without reviewing the details and reading between the lines.  *He works like a machine and does not know what he is doing. *All he cares about is making money not your well being. *The rating system requires at least one star but my fair rating is minus 100 stars because he ruined my life forever.
[4] I went to Dr. Lee because I had a "TMJ Disorder" and his website said he did a fellowship focusing on TMJ.  My jaw was locked to the point that I could only open it two finger widths. Dr. Lee did all that he could to treat it without surgery, which I liked.  He gave me a splint that I wore 24/7 for about 6 months and then just at night as the pain went away.  Every visit I had, Dr. Lee asked me how the pain was.  It was getting better every time.  Now there is no pain and I still wear the splint at night but my bite is very off.  None of my right teeth touch.  I can't rip or tear with my teeth.  My jaw still locks but it unlocks.  My new dentist suggested that I get braces now to realign my bite and hopefully relieve my jaw. I cannot speak on any other procedure that Dr. Lee performs but for problems with TMJ, go somewhere else.  The pain was treated but the problem persisted and led to other problems.
[5] A definite five star review for Dr Lee and his staff. My first impression was wow look at this man's credentials!  The office is immaculate!  I first met Dr Lee as I had an emergency consult for a damaged tooth.  I was provided my options.  I wanted an implant and really did not know much about it including the process.  The entire process would cost quite a bit of money and my insurance for the year had already been used up.  After the initial consult and detailed cost explanation which were very over whelming to me.   In the days to follow I conducted further investigations of other surgeons.   I was convinced that Dr Lee is the choice for my situation.  The surgery was handled beautifully.  Kathryn was so knowledgeable regarding cost, insurance and making sure I understood everything.  She was very patient with me.  The end result is that I have an implant and even though my dentist's lab created the tooth the final results are amazing.  The practice makes sure that you understand everything and are patient and kind.  I am very pleased with the whole process and each step of the way. I would not hesitate to go back if needed.
[6] First let me start by saying this star is not for doctor lee this is for his staff I was referred into his office by my dentist they scheduled me an appointment as soon as possible I get in and I sign all required documentation needed for me to start the consultation as I sit the office manager is gossiping with someone one behind the counter and then another girl comes in looking like she had a long night out if you know what I mean so I get taken back I’m sitting waiting and the office manager comes in and ask so did anyone speak with you about the payment process I said no and she started telling me what things would cost I told her I had $0 dollars which you would think then she would tell me then I’m sorry there is nothing we can do but no she lets me go on with my visit I get the X-ray done and dr lee comes in and tells me instead of the 3 teeth I was referred in to pull I need 5 and that he can do it today and I’m thinking great the pain and infection will finally be gone NOPE here comes billing after repeatedly telling them I have no money she said well do you have someone who can help I said no well if you can come up with $800 down we can set you up on a payment plan well I couldn’t do that so I left I then call later to say I might be able to do the $800 and payments she said oh is that what I told you well we will only be able to do 3 teeth I said why you quoted me for all 5 before I left she then say oh okay well instead of the $100 you will have to pay 250 a month mind you everyone this is to just have them pulled not replaced with new teeth I know everyone works to make a living and I’m not asking for free work but you couldn’t even help someone who has a really bad infection and needs 5 teeth pulled and can end up in the hospital what kind of people are you money isn’t everything in this world kindness I could have paid it back in time but greed has your minds messed up I will not refer anyone your way and there will be a bad review on every site possible so thank you for nothing!!!!!
[7] Dr.Lee & staff, I could not thank you guy enough. My experience was fantastic. It was pain free and I felt that you and the staff cared about my well being.Very professional, highly satisfied, Great PlaceThanks
[8] I was sent to Dr. Lee urgently, because during a routine tooth extraction, a drill bit went into my sinus cavity and got stuck. Dr. Lee was gracious enough to fit me into his busy Friday schedule to perform urgent oral surgery to remove the drill bit from my sinus cavity. Dr. Lee is very confident regarding his profession, he made me feel  comfortable going into surgery. He even had a joke or two when asking him if he had ever removed a drill bit. [1] Very quaint shop in Oxnard’s old Woolworth store. A little difficult to spot but oh so good!  The crepes and coffee are excellent. The owner is the nicest guy!!!  His wife hand makes the sauces for the crepes with all fresh ingredients. Absolutely terrific!  Prices are very reasonable. Wish I took pictures🤨. Mouth watering presentation!  Only down side is there are only a few tables and chairs. Place is very clean and well kept!  Can’t wait to take friends and go back myself!!  All the baked goods looked yummy. Definitely go!!!
[2] Great little family owned dessert shop in downtown Oxnard. All of their baked goods are super tasty, great coffee (especially the cajeta frappe!), and their crepes are delicious!
[3] I’ve been a regular customer for months now, and I can confidently say that Xielo Artisan Desserts is an excellent café!  Not only is the menu varied with delicious options for all palates, but they use high quality ingredients, have wonderful presentation, and keep surprising you as you continue to visit!  The owner, Juan, humbly shares that his family who put together their recipies are the artists, and essentially he’s just the business side making it available to people.  Well their family has been in the food industry for many years tracing back to Mexico, and they have recipies that his grandmother made 60 years ago! My favorites are the Berry French Cream sweet crepe with extra BFC, the albondigas soup (best I’ve ever had), and my custom cajeta & lechera, light ice, extra coffee frappe!  Five honorable mentions are the guayaba y queso empanadas, the cajeta con nuez sweet crepe with a scoop of ice cream on top, the monte alban sweet crepe, the requeson, and the cafe de olla (can you tell I have a sweet tooth? 🤣). The café has a relaxing atmosphere with the nice music and window-shop seating, and has been a nice place to do work, watch a movie on my iPad, or have interesting conversations with people in the shared seating area. Now, above all of this is their impressive customer service!  Early on I was welcomed como familia (like family), and they have continued to make me feel like an appreciated customer in several ways.  The owner Juan, his father (also Juan), his mother Aracely, his cousin Cynthia, and one of their main employees Iselda have all been great, whether on the phone or in person, remembering my usual order, asking how I’m doing, or handing me my order with a smile 😃👍.  Stop by this wonderful family run business; enjoy the tasty food and drinks, the hospitable family feel, and experience part of their heritage!  You’ll be glad that you did 😎

The chef that was making the cepes took the time to explain the variety of ingredients of the toppings for the crepes. The owner was also very nice and attentive. [1] My wife and I are escape room enthusiasts with over 20 rooms under our belts. We try to play at least one game whenever we travel. While we didn't escape from The House of Clues' Alien Spacecraft Invasion (correction: we escaped, but we didn't save the earth), we had a blast! The scenery in the room is awesome, the lights are disorienting (just like they should be on a UFO), and the puzzles are challenging! Plus, there's a surprise waiting in the room that made both me and my wife jump!My only complaint about this room was the number of similar locks. It's totally a preference thing, but I think a perfect escape room has only one of each type of lock at a time, so when I discover 3 digits or a key, I know exactly where it's supposed to go. Here, I had to test each combination in three or four different locks before finding the right one. That ate a lot of our time.We had a nice chat with the owner after playing and are very impressed with their business!Overall, we had an excellent time here and will be returning to play their Pirate Ghost Ship!

The organization of the clues was great and didnt force you into a clueless circle. The puzzles were difficult enough to get you thinking and get creative but easy enough so that you all dont just stand there not knowing what to do next. The props were so much fun to interact with and for at least the room I was in, they didnt try to bog you down with endless red herrings that get you stuck and unable to move forward. The price isnt bad for what it is, I would personally say it is worth paying the 35, load of fun. I cant wait to try another room!

My trust was broken.  I can't help but think they just put the old defected tire back on.

Nothing changed other than the balance in my checking account.All I wanted was a refund, seeing as they clearly didn't fix anything in my car, but my car is now unsafe to drive thanks to them, and they said they can't do anything without looking at it first. [6] Jason was super awesome. Great customer service. Changed my brakes and was a reasonable price. Definitely would come back again.
[7] Awesome service. Came late in the day with a nail in my tire and they took care of it without any issues!

I called in the morning with make of our minivan, model and tire sizes I needed, I spoke with Tony who told me he had 8 of the tires I needed and quoted me $109.00 each "installed", so I gave my Wife the green light to head down there because we had an upcoming road trip, we've used this Just Tires for years with success. Once she arrived the price on the tires doubled to over $200 per tire, I told her, "just leave" and she spoke with Tony while I held on the phone, suddenly, magically the tires were down to $109.00 again. How much profit margin do they have? Later my Wife texted me about an alignment and an oil change, looking at about $540.00 plus tax so I texted her "OK". Then once the car was done there were all these "extras" for valve stems, wheel balance, tire pressure sensors, I spoke with Tony and said, "What happened to the 'installed' price I was told this morning?", he went on to lament how "Goodyear sets the prices" and he has no control over all that. Then the more I spoke to him, and the more my Wife spoke he kept changing the prices. Suddenly the tires were down to $94.99 to absorb some of the balancing and other costs. So he has control or he doesn't have control? I mean which is it?In the end everything was very shady and we had to confront, kick and scream to get close to what we were originally told. There used to be an employee there named Noel, (not sure I spelled his name right I just remember the name) and he was a much more straight forward person, what he said was what happened and that's why we went back so often. I'll have to see if he is still there and deal with him, if he's at another location and go there, or just find a new place for tires, there are plenty on Tustin. We personally own 8 cars total, plus the inventory that passes through my Dealership that occasionally need tires, brakes, or other work done. I'm sure we can do better than this experience.

Real Customer Reviews (Total: 10):
[1] Beautiful views and we saw no mountain lions that the sign warned about!
[2] Love to get a breakfast there. Lunch menu is not so good. Beautiful view
[3] A bit steep, but worth doing. Really pretty. Usually not so crowded. Watch for snakes.
[4] Perfect short hike with a great reward! Took us about 45 minutes from start to finish and we could have gone a good deal faster. Wildflowers are beautiful in this small canyon and the view can’t be beat. Highly recommend!
[5] Short and challenging hike with rewarding views at the peak.
[6] Laguna Beach Cliff Hike is a must if you're more interested in the unique often missed gems in Laguna Beach OR just want a cool run with good views :) The trail is perfect distance! Once you get higher up you will have two routes actually three if your interested exploring east. If you go the natural route to the top you have two layers of trails. Both trails are awesome once you get to the top you get a clear somewhat steep view of what appears to be The Montage golf course. Watchout for mountain lions and rattlesnakes, etc.
[7] Hard up hill hije but so worth it! View is awesome!
[8] This is a favorite hike for our family with great views of Laguna Beach! There are two approaches and one is more difficult than the other. The south trailhead is our favorite, but is more difficult. It begins in a residential neighborhood and ascends something like 600-650 feet in a matter of a 1/4-1/2 mile. With kids, this might be a little difficult. The other approach is from the East and is much more moderate because you park on top of a hill. When you come to the fork in the trail near the end, keep right to ascend to the tallest part of the peak. The views are excellent and there is a bench at the top. Beware of the sharp cliffs with the kids!
[9] A very beautiful hike. I come here to get away and escape. Not too challenging, but a good amount of distance.
[10] This hike showed me how out of shape I am. 10/10 would do again.

User Question: What are the key features and safety considerations for the Laguna Beach Cliff Hike based on customer experiences?

The Laguna Beach Cliff Hike offers beautiful and rewarding views, with some customers describing it as a must-visit for unique gems in Laguna Beach [1][2][4][5][6][7][8]. The hike is generally short, taking about 45 minutes from start to finish, and can be challenging due to steep sections [3][4][5][7]. There are multiple trail options, including two main approaches: a more difficult south trailhead that ascends 600-650 feet in a short distance, and a more moderate east approach from the top of a hill [6][8]. At the fork near the end, keeping right leads to the tallest part of the peak [8]. Safety considerations include warnings about mountain lions, rattlesnakes, and sharp cliffs, especially for families with children [1][3][6][8]. The trail is usually not crowded, and wildflowers add to the beauty in the small canyon [3][4].

Truck drivers report significant issues with the loading process and facilities at True Leaf Farms. The loading process is often slow, with drivers experiencing long wait times, sometimes up to 15 hours, and delays in getting assigned a dock [3][5][6][10]. Appointments are required but may not result in timely loading or dock access [2][3][5]. The facility is described as unorganized and congested, with limited parking for only about 8 trucks and problems with farm trucks blocking access [1][2]. Communication is poor, as there is no live person at the check-in point, requiring phone calls to reach anyone, and appointments must be made in person to obtain a phone number [2]. Staff on the phone can be hateful, and the facility prioritizes dropped trailers over waiting drivers [8]. Facilities are inadequate, with dirty portable plastic restrooms and no proper sanitary toilets [1][9]. The area becomes muddy in rain and dusty otherwise, and specific docks like 11 and 12 may damage truck trailers [1][9]. Inconsistencies in loading procedures are noted, such as drivers being asked to vacate docks due to lack of quality control personnel, while others receive preferential treatment [10]. Despite these issues, some positive interactions are mentioned, such as a helpful shag driver and a loader who assisted with cleaning and securing loads [2][4].
