[1] The smell of fresh baked bread will tempt you and if that isn't enough they put on a window show so you can watch as they prepare their yummy goodness live and send out samples. That was enough for us to walk in and sit down for lunch.The wait was 20 minutes so we took a tour of the gift shop and then headed upstairs where we noticed there was a museum tour for $3 however we watched from above as the flour is brought in on an assembly line, dumped into a large vat, mixed with eggs, water, etc, and then prepared into bread. That was pretty neat and made the wait time go by fast.We got a lovely window seat next to a couple from the East coast and watched street traffic while waiting for service. We also learned that everyone around us was here to run the race including our waiter. Go figure.I ordered the chicken club sandwich with shoe string fries. The order was prompt but we never got our complimentary bread until the end of the meal. Oh well. The sandwich itself had a juicy chicken breast, crisp bacon, ripe pieces of avocado, and crisp romaine lettuce. It was so unbelievably yummy. After having walked up and down Fisherman's Wharf, I was starving so I ate my sandwich and half of my fries in about 15 minutes and before the waiter could come back and check up on us.I also loved that during the race Boudin was handing out bread. They were at mile 1 with bread baskets. I grabbed a few handfuls and ate them during my marathon. That was awesome!
[2] ordered online here and got the wrong order. someone else is enjoying my clam chowder in a bread bowl right now while im eating their chili in a bread bowl.....
[3] This is your go to place for any food that can include sourdough bread. Their sourdough sandwiches are delicious and so our their clam chowder bread bowls. It's really fast and good to come to after work. It's not too pricey either.

They also have a cute bread "museum" inside, along with a viewing platform to see how the bread is made. It was a bit too tourist-y for my taste, but it's fun to do just once.
[7] Boudin's a great staple in SF. The bread is excellent as expected but the items on the menu are also great. For lunch, the grilled cheese with turkey paired with a tomato soup is the way to go. The portions are generous and the quality is high. They also serve a good and fast breakfast. They offer bagels as well as breakfast sandwiches. The breakfast sandwiches are cheap and very large. They offer a full breakfast at a rapid pace. This is definitely a good staple for someone that works downtown.
[8] The Clam Chowder is AMAZING. Worth coming here. Do try the bread bowl clam chowder, it's worth every cent.

[1] The epitome of a trendy hipster SF bar. I didn't order any food here but had a couple cocktails. They were alright, not as much of a selection as you might expect from a speakeasy- type place. 5 stars for a total commitment to atmosphere and feel. 4.5 stars for drinks.
[2] Very nice place with good food and service. Spacious and cozy at the same time. We had octopus, yellow fish tartar and Maine lobster for starters, all were great! The prime courses were ok. The desserts were fantastic! And I got a birthday cake what was nice!
[3] Shandy's is my absolute favorite bar. Completely unpretentious, a great mix of people and reasonably priced drinks. If you really want to experience Shandy's at it's finest be sure to go on a weekday as the weekends are always crowded.Everyone here is friendly, including the bartenders. My one and only gripe about Shandy's would be the amount of people that just HAVE to play "Bela Lugosi's Dead" by Bauhaus over and over and over. Sure, it's the only Bauhaus song you've ever heard but it's the worst song to play at a bar. So please, stay away from the Bauhaus album in the jukebox, the regulars will thank you.
[4] We were actually staying in the hotel here and stopped by both nights we were there. The patio is covered in adverse weather, but still nice and inviting, and you can smoke out there. They have a selection of cigars available to buy inside too if that's your thing.The prices are decent for Beverley Hills, beer was under $10 dollars, and drinks were good. We were in travel clothes (nice jeans and a sweater) both nights, and we weren't looked down upon, even though I felt severely underdressed. This is great for people watching!! Occasionally you might catch a star or 2 on the weekends.They have a great selection of food as well. Sliders, pizza, sushi, sandwiches, a little bit of everything. They're open late til around 3am too. Food under $30. Just a nice relaxed atmosphere all the way around, and super helpful non pretentious staff.
[5] Nicest upscale bar on the strip. Hands down. Beautiful pool area with great views, seating and/or cabanas depending on what time of day or year you come. Either way, it's great. If you're looking to impress on a first date, you will not fail here. If it doesn't impress, say I had a great day/evening and move on. Four different areas, all connected but with completely separate and distinct vibes. All great. Excellent cocktail, wine and bubbles selection at the bar above the pool. DJs and pool parties held here often. This is technically not a rooftop bar however it is roofless on good days and it is elevated 2-3 stories. Though only the elite stay and hang out here, everyone is welcome. Highly recommended anytime of the day, year-round.
[6] Amazing Place laid-back great drinks had an amazing time with my friends definitely would recommend this place if you're coming from out of town or simply just live in Los Angeles need to spend on something different for a night-time affair
[7] I'm in a band and this is one of my favorite places to play.  The sound is amazing, service is incredible, and the people there are so friendly.  It's the best place in Pomona if you're just stopping by for a drink, to check out a show, or to play a show, I highly recommend.
[8] Very trendy spot.  Not for a quiet romantic dinner!  The seating area is open and can be very noisy.  Food was good, service was good.
[9] I have dined here a few times, but my main attraction is their lovely lounge and bar area downstairs.  The cozy center fireplace, intimate setting, and lovely modern and sleek decor makes 25 Lusk a nice hot spot for date drinks and also intimate dinners.  Excellent service and friendly staff.
[10] Absolutely love this place. I chose to come here instead of a club for my birthday this year. I'm so happy I made that decision because it was so nice, elegant, and perfect wine environment to have an actual conversation.

messages.content: You are a professional local business analyst. Please objectively and accurately answer user questions about a specific business based on the provided context. Be honest if you don't know.

Business Name: Cali Bamboo
Category: Building materials supplier, Deck builder, Fence supply store, Flooring store, Wood and laminate flooring supplier
Description: Specialist stocking bamboo flooring, fencing, decking & plywood, plus wall coverings & mattings.

Real Customer Reviews (Total: 10):
[1] I am very pleased with our new Cali floor. The quality of finish and overall appearance are excellent.
[2] Jimmy Gutierrez was very helpful, made sure we ordered exactly what we needed and kept us up to date on shipping. We’ve ordered twice from him and will do so again.
[3] I had never installed clickable flooring before.  To my surprise, the Cali vinyl flooring was very easy to install.  At the ends of rows, I had to cut pieces (using the leftover part at the far end of the next row).  I mainly employed simple handtools (tape rule, pencil, utility knife, two c-clamps and metal straightedge to guide the blade, and cut-proof gloves, of course).  Once a plank was scored, it snapped easily (for scores across the grain).  For scoring parallel to the long dimension, a board placed under the plank was used to help in snapping it.  Once the first row was laid along the long wall, it was simple to snap in the successive rows, beginning with a shorter piece so as to stagger the joints.  It is important to make sure that the planks are snugly fitted to the preceding row, but if they are not, you'll see that it doesn't lie down flat.  A short piece of 1x2 can be set along the edge of the plank and tapped with a small hammer to assist the fitting.  I installed the flooring in 3 rooms, including a closet and a pantry, and also fitted the planks around the base of permanent wardrobes-type cabinets.  For each room, removing the existing carpet and tack strips, patching the concrete subfloor (skim coat) and installing the underlayment took about 2 days and laying the vinyl only 1 day.  Finishing with quarter-round and transition molding took another half-day.  It looks great!Before buying this product, I had tested Cali's free samples against several other similar products by placing them under a heavy chair which I weighted down with boxes of books.  I saw dents in the surface of many of the samples, but none in the Cali samples!  This convinced me that this is a superior product which should last decades.The product arrived on time and the truck driver helped me pull the pallet jack into my garage.  All of the order was neatly shrink-wrapped on a pallet and the individual boxes were all in good shape with no dents or tears.  Each box of planks weighs 51 pounds, so getting them into the condo was a chore (1700 pounds overall)!I had very little waste (mainly due to "ripping" planks for the last row in each room).  I had ordered 2 extra boxes (5% overage), but did not use them.  They will be given to my son who is replacing flooring in a utility room and hallway.
[4] I love my new cali bamboo distressed floor. It looks great!
[5] What we bought: approximately 800 square feet of Distressed Mocha Fossilized bamboo flooring and an equal amount of cork underlayment.Where we put it: great room and kitchen with open-floor plan, two hallways, and an office adjacent to the kitchenOverall Review: We have animals, a young son, and plan to stay in the house for a while, so we wanted something durable yet beautiful and classic. My husband discovered Cali Bamboo on the web while searching for flooring, and we were a little suspicious at first because no prices were listed, but we loved their products, and submitted an online inquiry. Within less than 24 hours, I was on the phone with Andrew, who is one of the most informative, kind, and honest salespeople I’ve ever dealt with. After speaking with Andrew, we immediately decided to commit to buying our floors from Cali Bamboo even though we did not feel any pressure to buy anything nor any pressure to keep our order should we have changed our minds.When we ordered, we had a few months before installation, so we were able to provide a down payment but to hold off shipment. In the meantime, we spoke with Andrew regularly to make sure we had ordered the correct amount and to ask various questions that came up as we moved closer to installation time. I can’t express enough how helpful it was to deal with one person and to feel as though we could contact him anytime with any questions. There was never any delay in his response, and we always felt as though our questions were answered sufficiently and effectively.Quality and process: The flooring is absolutely beautiful. We are so pleased with the distressed mocha. There is such an amazing color variation in the pieces that everything goes with it. The flooring is also very durable and has already sustained a few hard knocks with installation, but it does not damage easily. Because of its durability, the flooring is quite heavy and quite a job to unpack and set up for installation, but that is to be expected.We had very few pieces that were unusable. In fact, I think only one or two pieces were splintered a bit, so overall, we were able to use every bit of what we ordered despite the shipping process and just how heavy the wood is. We were also successful not wasting much when cutting pieces to fit.In our installation process, we encountered a few issues that are not reflective of the flooring itself, but I thought it might help to mention for self-installers: the flooring we ordered was so thick that we had to shave the bottoms of all of our door frames and doors, which made the installation process take much longer. If you are replacing carpet or laminate, cut out a piece near your door frames and measure the new flooring and underlayment to make sure the flooring fits underneath. If you can do some of this work ahead of time, you will save time when you actually begin installing. We used an oscillating multi-tool for this task, and it made the cutting much easier. We also had to adjust the height of our appliances for the same reason. We wanted thick flooring, and we got it! :)The cork underlayment is relatively thick and is not easy to cut, so make sure to get a good supply of utility blades and change them out frequently. This will make the cutting much easier. We also realized that it’s best to only put one or two rows of underlayment at a time. This may be obvious to experienced installers, but we didn’t realize how easily the underlayment could get torn up or moved around if we put more than two pieces together until we had made the mistake twice. You may also want to get more underlayment than you think you’ll need because you end up having to waste some of it when cutting it to fit.Overall, everything about the purchasing experience, the quality of the flooring, and the style of the flooring was top-notch and beyond our expectations. We are so very pleased we decided to go with Cali Bamboo, and we have received so many compliments on our flooring. It’s been a wonderful experience!
[6] We absolutely love this product!  It is very high quality, very durable, easy to install, and looks beautiful.  In addition, the Sales and Support were very helpful during and after the order process.
[7] Cali Bamboo was wonderful to work with from start to finish.  My Sells Rep, Jodi Rizzi listened to my ideas and worked with me all the way through the purchasing process.  I did the install myself.  It was an easy project to complete.
[8] We ordered bamboo fencing. Quality is excellent, sales staff extremely helpful. We had 2 panels damaged by the freight company, no fault of Cali Bamboo. Angela in customer service was very helpful, replaced the panels no charge, shipped quickly to us so we could finish our project. Angela repeatedly followed up all along the way to ensure our complete satisfaction. This is what customer service looks like! I highly recommend this firm, and, Cali Bamboo is lucky to have an employee like Angela.
[9] I've had the opportunity to deal directly with the major bamboo brands, and Calibamboo has consistently offered the best products and service.I requested samples for both strandwoven plywood and flooring from each of the brands, and have come away impressed with Calibamboo's products. Their strandwoven flooring is denser than other competitors and much harder when rapped with the knuckles. I rested the sample floorboard strip on two bamboo blocks and was thoroughly surprised that the strip did not flex one bit when I stood one-legged on it. It did not yield even when I bounced my entire 165lbs on it.Moreover, Calibamboo's strandwoven plywood has thicker layers of strandwoven bamboo than other competitors. Given that the strandwoven bamboo is an order of strength greater than natural bamboo, this is indeed a great buy. It does not help that Calibamboo is cheaper than its major competitors.However, what sealed the deal for me was the level of service and competency that Calibamboo provided despite my tiny order. From the get go, the sales rep I first spoke with over the hotline personally followed up two days after I received my samples, and continued doing so even after making it known that I needed no more than a sheet of plywood. Furthermore, I received a direct call from Laura, a departmental head, who personally worked with me to find the optimal way to ship that single sheet to Missouri. As a major supplier, they did not have to do any of this for me. The inordinate amount of time and sincerity they have offered is in stark contrast with, for example, the other major Californian supplier that sent me down their distributor chain with reps I could never contact throughout the week. And still Calibamboo was cheaper and better.My only suggestion is that Calibamboo should make a plywood entirely of strandwoven bamboo like their flooring. If they manage to achieve a similar 5000 Janka rating on that plywood, I believe many uninitiated individuals and businesses would give bamboo a look for products from DIY to furniture to speaker manufacturers.I respect their products, and appreciate their sincere service, and will continue looking to Calibamboo for solutions. They have been a pleasure to work with!
[10] Excellent presale service. Very patient and helpful until we found the flooring and that was just right. Material is excellent and the price was a little less than all the other competitors.

User Question: What are the key features and benefits of Cali Bamboo's flooring products based on customer experiences?

Cali Bamboo's flooring products are praised for their high quality and durability, with customers noting excellent finish and appearance [1][4]. The flooring is described as very durable, able to sustain hard knocks without damage, and some customers expect it to last decades [3][5]. It is also easy to install, with customers finding the clickable vinyl flooring simple to lay using basic hand tools [3][6][7]. The products offer aesthetic appeal, with beautiful color variation and a classic look that receives many compliments [4][5]. Additionally, the flooring is noted for being denser and harder than competitors' products, with strandwoven flooring showing no flex under weight [9]. Customers appreciate the minimal waste during installation and the availability of free samples for testing [3][5][9]. The sales and support staff, including representatives like Jimmy Gutierrez, Andrew, Jodi Rizzi, and Angela, are highlighted as very helpful, informative, and responsive throughout the purchasing process [2][5][6][7][8][10]. The company provides good customer service, with timely shipping and assistance in handling orders, even for small purchases [2][3][8][9][10]. Prices are competitive, often being a little less than other competitors [10].

address: Norwegian Airline, 209 World Way, Los Angeles, CA 90045

messages.content: You are a professional local business analyst. Please objectively and accurately answer user questions about a specific business based on the provided context. Be honest if you don't know.

Business Name: Norwegian Airline
Category: Airline

Real Customer Reviews (Total: 10):
[1] Thieves who try to weigh everything possible even food. Shame on you. Never again with you
[2] Planes are dirty - staff is both inexperienced and rude and all the cost cutting means when you land a bus will shuttle you to the terminal, bags will take forever ensuring your trip feels as long as it possible can
[3] It's a shame that you can't do zero stars for a review. This airline is by far the worst I've ever dealt with. Be aware of missinformation online.Travelling with pets is not allowed on international flights even though it doesn't specify that on the webpage. It says that for DOMESTIC flights they allow certain countries, but nowhere it says they don't allow pets for international flights, or for any flight from the USA, even though in the US Norwegian Airline Webpage it gives you the option to add your pet online or on the phone.We bought our flight looking at this info and when we tried to add our cat online it didn't let us, so we bought the flight thinking to add it on the phone later as instructed. They told us it cannot be done and also they will not give us a refund, even though our flight is not for a few weeks.Both on the phone and on chat they didn't help us and said they cannot transfer to management.Also, we purchased a more expensive option than the cheaper one, "flex"...this didn't help at all with refunds or any other possible help.NEVER CHOOSE THIS AIRLINE! If I had seen the reviews before purchasing I would have never bought tickets with them. This is what you pay for. This is the worst airline ever, and customer service is the worst. I had to wait for a long time both on the phone and on chat until somebody responded.There is a Facebook page called: "Norwegian Airlines Experiences from Hell"...why do you think this exists??
[4] Reserved and paid a seat, but at the LAX airport they told in a rude way that it's not available.
[5] Norwegian is the absolute worst. There is a reason there is an entire Facebook community dedicated to "Norwegian experiences from Hell". Every single flight with them has had issues, especially with customer service but this last experience takes the cake.Flight from Newark to Rome scheduled to leave at 11:30, first we were delayed getting on the plane by an hour. We get on the plane and get situated (with a baby who is dead tired) and 2 HOURS after sitting on the plane we are told over the intercom that a vehicle crashed into the luggage door and they were trying to fix it but they won't be able to so our flight is being cancelled. We then sit on the plane for another hour while they "make accommodations" for everyone since it is 3am. Well when we get off the plane and get to baggage claim there is no customer service representatives and they just tell everyone to "go home" well 90% of us could not go home so we asked the flight attendants and they said our best option was to get a hotel and they would call us to rebook our flight for the next day. So we paid OUT OF POCKET for a hotel, transportation, etc.The next day we did not receive a call so we called and spent over 3 hours on hold (this is on the "phone line dedicated to our cancellation") just to be told that they didn't have any direct flights like the one we paid extra money to book originally until 3 days later and that they didn't have "clearance" to get us on a direct flight that day so our only option was to take a flight leaving at midnight that night with a 4 hr layover in Spain.We accepted the flight since it was our only option but of course it was a huge inconvenience as we had to pay for another day at the hotel and Babies naps were all messed up from the insanity so the flight was pretty terrible but to make it worse they kept the overhead lights in the plane on for pretty much the entire 9 hr flight so my daughter struggled to sleep. When I asked when they would turn the lights off they said when people were done eating (this was 1am NY time). Funny because I thought that was the whole reason they have individual lights on seats- so that the entire flight doesn't have to suffer.Here is the worst part though: we file our claim so we can get reimbursed and even on their website it says that if a flight is delayed more than 6 hours you are entitled to 600 euro per reservation. Well more than a month after the issue they get back to us basically saying that because it was caused by "circumstances which could not have been avoided" they won't be compensating us.How is an employee crashing into the luggage door not something that is preventable? How about pay more attention or don't go to work drunk? I cannot believe that Norwegian would not take responsibility for such a complete disaster. I will never be flying with them again and I would not recommend them to anyone.Oh and here is a bonus: on our flight home Rome to Newark I was denied a cup of water for the entire 9 hr flight and my "attendant" light was ignored the entire flight even though other people around us were given water when they asked and I am a nursing mother with a baby. Totally ridiculous.
[6] Never had a reason to actually rate an airline, but this one was terrible. We were flying from London Gatwick to Los Angeles and when I tried to check-in at the kiosk, it would not read my passport. Whatever, no big deal I’ll just check in at the counter. We encountered a young woman who was asking us the regular security questions about our bags being unattended, etc.. She asked if I had any sharp objects in my bag, I responded with “yes” because I had some old WW2 bullets that were recovered from Omaha Beach from Normandy. Now I’m a history teacher back home, so it was a very important and meaningful artifact to me. They had been drained of any gunpowder, linked together so as to prevent them from ever being loaded into a gun, overall completely safe. We had ZERO trouble with previous Airlines, transporting these across Europe, but these agents immediately got VERY snippy and smart with me. They kept asking me where they were from (I had told them three times), what was my purpose in having them, and then another agent started asking me about my profession and why these would be important to me, then denied me countless times. I had to drop them at a mail off kiosk.Already extremely annoyed, we went trough the process of weighing our bags at the counter. My husband and I had three small bags between the two of us and unknown to me, each person is only allowed 10kg to carry on. My bag was 7, his was 13. So we started stuffing clothes into my bag and slowly got to the allowed weight. By their graciousness and mercy (🙄), they were approved. So we weighed our check in bags, each having to be only 20kg (honestly though?? What is with Europe and these TEENY bag allowances, I miss America). We were both over by a combined weight of 5 kg which we were forced to pay 45£ for!! FORTY FIVE POUNDS FOR 5 KG!!! What a complete JOKE!! I get that they’re cheaper, but what made it even worse was the attendant who was just berating us with insanely pricey offers of checking a third bag and paying 9£ for each kg. Just awful.I will never fly this airline again, I hardly think I’m ever flying back to Europe again. Would have rather paid more for a better airline to get the service I expect than to be treated like that ever again.**EDIT**I will say that the flight itself was wonderful. The crew was accommodating, kind, and very helpful. The flight was enjoyable and the aircraft was very clean and modern. It was the preflight interaction that left a bad first impression. Maybe they should check the attitudes of those representing them on the ground. The flight crew was impeccable, too bad it wasn’t the same for the check in crew.
[7] The value being offered here is undeniable. Unfortunately their unreliable service and constant lateness in departures and arrivals is unacceptable.  Decent planes but the buck stops there. They're an embarrassement to the airline industry and a joke.
[8] The worst airline I have ever been on.My trip was from Barcelona-Los Angeles 11 hours direct. I paid my ticket, but they never notified me that snacks, drinks, headphones were not included. As I start my journey, I realize that you have to buy everything from water to headphones. these conditions are unacceptable for such a long and direct flight.Tips if you choose this airline to travel. take your things.
[9] I have flown Norwegian to Europe twice  in the past year. It is less expensive than other airlines and the on-line booking and alerts are excellent.  But I may not do it again. The planes are new Boeing 787 Dreamliners, but with 9 seats per row, little front space and almost no recliner, a 10 hours flight is eternal. The video entertainment  is ok. We bought the "tasty" food package. It is not better than the non-tasty food of other airlines. The crew is indifferent, look tired or anoyed, and service did not make them happier. Pouring coffee without looking is always alarming; luckily the coffee was cold. The gates locations are at the end of the airport, with a long ride to the plane in a packed, hot bus, where we were treated by the crew like catle, complete with shouting and pressing those of us inside to accommodate more passengers, what was obviously difficult. So all the positive aspects were overshadowed by the shortcomings. I must admit that the competition with other larger, better known airlines is healthy, but they should not try to imitate them.
[10] Finally taking the time to write a review of our trip that we took to Europe last year 09/05/15 via Norwegian air . After some of the complaints we read about on Norwegian Air we were a little worried after we booked. But once we flew them we were beyond pleasantly surprised!Keep in mind we also drove from Reno to Oakland and we still loved our experience with Norwegian Air.We flew out 09/05/15 from Oakland, CA (USA) to Oslo, Norway cheaper than I could fly from Reno, NV to Las Vegas, NV.Check in was quick and efficient as well as polite and respectful. We purchased one way cheap economy ticketsThe plane was large, new, clean and crowded. But that what you get when traveling on a budget.We booked early so my 6"4' husband had leg room near an emergency exit. I and my children are shorter than my husband and my daughter who is 5"7' felt she had enough leg room.Each seat had a TV screen with games etc. in the seat ahead of them. These entertainment consoles are free to use unless you need to purchase headphones. My family brought headphones and we all played games, listened to music, watched downloaded TV programs or movies. This was all offered for free. I greatly appreciate this because in the old days there were one maybe 2 movies on an overseas flight and you didn't get to choose what you wanted it was chosen for you.The console also let you see in real time where the airplane was in location to your destination. I thought this was pretty cool so did my son.Each seat has a USB port and you can charge your phone or other device.We paid extra for the meals and luggage. It was so worth the extra money.  I was pleasantly surprised by the quality of food, the taste the appeal. I had curry chicken and rice with veggies I can't remember what the desert was but it was tasty. Then we were served a sandwich.The airline attendants were super friendly and sweet. All we had was carry on. We did not experience any issues or difficulties with our trip. As a matter of fact we flew three more times within Europe on this one trip via other Airlines and my whole family agrees we would fly Norwegian Air again anytime. I traveled with my husband, 18 year old daughter and 13 year old son.Thanks Norwegian Air for making it possible for a family to afford to travel to Europe again!!

User Question: What should I know about the difficulty and safety considerations for hiking the Tenaja Falls Trail?

The Tenaja Falls Trail is a beautiful hiking spot that leads to a tall waterfall with an overlook of the entire trail [3]. The hike is about 4.5 miles in one way, making it a 9-mile round trip from the trailhead to the falls [4]. It is not a very far or difficult walk overall, with a consistent elevation change and some rocky areas to navigate [5][4]. However, there are safety considerations: the path is single walker wide with areas where a slip could cause falls of several hundred feet, potentially fatal, so caution is advised, especially with small children [4]. There are dangerous ledges and wildlife in the area, including Mountain Lions and Rattlesnakes, requiring proper precautions [3][4]. Additionally, there is no cellular service, so plan accordingly [4]. The trail includes river crossings, which may be difficult if there is a lot of water, but can be navigated using skipping stones or a rope found a little farther down [5][10]. It is recommended to go during the rainy season or after rain to see the river and waterfall flowing [8][10].

[9] Too bad there isn't a 6 star rating. What can you say that hasn't been said about Bijan? Speaking not as a visitor but a resident of Beverly Hills for many years, this is the only boutique that always stood out from the rest and represented the heritage of 90210. Opened in 1976, this is the last of the original locally owned boutiques, and continues to carry the torch that Fred Hayman lit way back in the day. Founder and designer Bijan Pakzad was a local legend and the man who raised the bar as high as it could go. After his untimely passing in 2011, his son Nicolas carried on that tradition with true class and character. A very down to earth, family run business, yet featuring the most intricately beautiful and expensive clothing you will find anywhere on earth. And on a personal note, when our kids were doing their annual school fundraiser at the local elementary school, House of Bijan was the only local business that contributed. What a class act all the way around. I must end this review with a simple congratulations to Nicolas Bijan himself. Being the son of a legendary designer and inheriting the business is no small task - one might assume it would be hard to step out of his father's shadow. Such as not been the case. Yes, he carries on the memory of his father with grace and respect, but he has stepped into his own light and made Bijan his own over these past nine years. Bijan will be around for a long time, and that's a nice feeling. Long Live Bijan!

[1] I really wanted to give the folks at BoxDog a chance, as i had heard great things about their service department,. Unfortunately, the service i received was quite poor.In March, I brought a brand new fork in to have BoxDog install it, cut the steerer, and install and bleed the hydraulic brake. The drop-off process was pretty straight forward. I brought the bike in mocked up as i wanted it, discussed it with the owner, and left. When i came back a few days later to pick it up, they had not followed my instructions, and they cut the fork too short for my needs. Frustrating, but understandable that there are sometimes communication issues between the person checking the bike in and the person performing the work. Problem was, the fork that i  had supplied was the last one available in the US for many weeks. After much communication with the owner, he agreed to replace the fork for me at no charge, as long as i paid for the labor when i picked it up. I paid $130 for the install and took my bike home for the long wait for the replacement fork.At the end of May, i got an email from the owner saying that my fork had arrived and i should drop my bike off to have it installed. Again, easy drop off and no issues. Had a nice conversation with the owner when i dropped it off. He mentioned that he was glad that i had brought the bike back to them so that they could make it right, instead of just finding a new shop to work with. I asked if they would swap the front rack and wired headlight over to the new fork, and he said it would be no problem.The next day, i got a call that my bike was ready and went to pick it up. When i got the bike, everything LOOKED correct. I was, however, shocked to find that the owner was charging me an additional $65 for the headlight installation, which was never mentioned to me at any point in time. I was not happy, but felt that i had to pay in order to get my bike back. The owner “kindly” gave me a 50% discount on the additional labor that they were chiseling out of me.I rode the bike across town and immediately put it in the stand to check the work. No offense to the shop, but i no longer trusted their work without checking it myself. I was quite disappointed to again find incredibly sloppy and unsafe work. The steerer was again cut shorter than i had requested.Worst of all, every single rack mounting bolt was hand tight. If they had worked their way loose, i would be facing a serious accident, or possibly death.I emailed the owner to let him know that i found the work shoddy, and that they had allowed a bike to leave the shop patently unsafe. I still have not received a response from the owner. In the end, BoxDog thinks that the sloppy work they performed is worth nearly $200. I’ve included photos of both forks as i got them from the shop. I’ve shared the photos with other bike shop employees, and to a one, they have said that it is poor work.Their shop has a number of nice things to sell, but i would not recommend their service department. There appears to be no process of checking over work to ensure it has been done safely.

[1] For Mellow Me Out, I have one song stuck in my head “Don’t be fooled by the rocks”. My sister in law booked this spa place for our girls gathering. I was totally looking forward for a heavenly experience. At first, I thought the spa was located in Fair Oaks inside the Arden hotel. I must be day dreaming and got my venues mixed up. My dreams shattered into glasses when I saw the location of the venue. If you are reading this review, Mellow me Out is located behind the McDonalds. For a first time comer, this place can be little overwhelming with the area.We reached the spa at 2pm, checked-in and they guided us to the waiting room. Not the best looking waiting room but it was descent.  After that, we were escorted into the locker room. In the locker room, most of the lockers were either occupied or not working. They have three changing rooms inside the locker room; they could only accommodate three people at a time. Six of us had to share two lockers. We didn’t mind sharing but what happens when there are so many people? They informed us we could change our clothes into the robes. When I glanced at the ropes, I was a little torn apart. They were old and did not have the plush feeling. They provided us flip flops but it was hurting our delicate feet’s. I know we do not come from palaces but we’re paying $110 that’s without the gratuity.Afterwards, we went to the lounge, there was a mermaid on the background. It was dark with bright colors and lights were dimmed. After we sat down, they filled the bowl with water and hot stones. It felt nice and warm and within few minutes the water became cold. I wish they had added flowers to the water to add a little excitement to our spa experiences. We had booked a spa package thinking, we’ll have the lounge to ourselves but that wasn’t the case here. We had to share with random strangers. And we were allowed to bring wine and snacks but it was a common lounge, it felt strange leaving behind food unattended. And they didn’t set up the table as expected for our wine prior to arrival. I was a little disappointed; the business advertises for the spa packages but not prepared to provide a thrilling experience.From our package deal, we had the option of choosing two things: Swedish massage, facial or pedicure. Each person was guided into their respective rooms separately.  I was the last one taken for the massage. My masseuses took me to the massage room next to the chiropractor office. I didn’t know they had a chiropractor at Mellow Me Out. My massage experience wasn’t the best. The guy did not massage my lower back and hands. During the massage, he asked if I wanted my legs massaged too. I replied, yes. The massage on the upper shoulder areas was great. He applied pressure on few stress points but the massage was less than 45 minutes. Oh no, I was ripped off on my time. They took me into the room late. After massage, they lead you back to the waiting room before the next service. I waited additional 5 minutes for my facial therapist. Anyways, my facial therapist was on a mission to keep applying and removing different products on my face. . However, she did not perform any dermalogical and extractions. I was totally saddened. [1] I recently had an exam and cleaning here. All staff members, from reception to dentists were very professional and caring. I highly recommend Park Place Dental for your dental care.

And when you do get a procedure done, it is not done properly with the blame being put on the patient for not flossing or taking care of the teeth. For example, I had my wisdom teeth removed and my entire gum separated from my teeth due to improper stitching confirmed by a second and third doctor. I did not need to have my wisdom teeth removed or need a cap or have gum graphing done.
[6] Everyone was great. If you ever need a dentist, look "NO FURTHER"

When I complained, they told me I would need root canals.I was shocked and took all my records to a top dentist, highly recommended, in San Francisco. Business Name: Yogurtland Los Angeles
Category: Frozen yogurt shop, Ice cream shop
Description: Outpost of a local chain offering self-serve frozen yogurt & toppings in a modern yet casual space.

Real Customer Reviews (Total: 10):
[1] I scream, you scream, after 10 pm I always scream for Yogurtland!Yes, almost every time we go out to dinner, my night is not complete without a trip to Yogurtland. If there’s not one nearby, I’ll have my husband drive until we find one. This particular location was clean, bright and (YaY) had the Tart flavor, which sets it apart.  There was a pretty good selection with most of the machine in order - often you’ll find many not serving, which lowers the choice of flavors significantly. I love to mix flavors, so I had chocolate, cheesecake and tart.  Now for the toppings.  Ok, so I don’t use any, but my daughter piles on the sprinkles.  Let me see what I can remember from the large selection; there are (obviously) sprinkles, crushed oreos, crushed/chopped nuts, crushed candy bars, cherries, strawberries, chocolate chips, gummies, M&Ms, gummie worms, coconut, bananas, pineapple, and...well, you get the idea.  I believe they usually have waffle cones, but I’m not certain; I’ve always purchased my yogurt in a cup.  If you’ve ever been to Yogurtland you know that the yogurt and toppings are sold by the ounce so it can get pricey.  Bottom line?  I 110% recommended Yogurtland for your next yogurt, ice cream, or dessert craving!!!
[2] I like this place because sometimes it has cookies and cream yogurt but sometimes they don't have it so they should have it more.
[3] Yummy for sure but $19.00 for two yougurts is too much. That's why I gave it 4 stars.
[4] This is by far the best yogurtland around, do u know why? They know what works and don't try and mess with the formula really if your I to frozen Yogurt and your in the area you should treat yourself to this delight and I'm sure it will really improve your day
[5] Classic Yogurtland, delicious yogurt inside a very clean store. Parking is a little bit of a pain.
[6] How sad that the employees including the manager just told us that no one blonde haired works here when we found a long blonde hair in his yogurt bowl. No apologies, nothing but manager said “it is what it is, it’s not ours it’s appetantly on the toppings”😳🤢🤧😵
[7] Theres one  thing I could say, its "WOW!" I was astounded by the flavors of yougurt they offered!!!
[8] Friendly staff. Good yogurt. Good selection of toppings, although not all the options are always consistent.
[9] Perfect reward after hiking at Runyon! My friend took me here to try some of there infamous ice cream macaroons. So glad she did because they were amazing!! They have a great selection of different flavors. Can't wait to try them all! Tried their Thai tea, cookies and cream and salted caramel flavors. Favorite so far is the salted caramel. Will probably try the grasshopper next :) This place does draw a crowd so you may have to wait in line for a bit. You can find parking on the side street (Poinsettia Place).
[10] Drove all the way from Downey to try Mashti Malone's based on the Yelp reviews, and it was totally worth it! I had 2 scoops and that was plenty. Had the peanut butter cup and cookies and cream. This ice cream was BOMB.COM!!!!! The peanut butter cup was the perfect amount of sweet and salty, and tasted so fresh! You could totally taste the the peanut butter. The cookies and cream was the best cookies and cream ice cream I've ever had. There was a ton of Oreo cookies in it and super creamy!

Yogurtland Los Angeles offers a variety of yogurt flavors, including tart, chocolate, cheesecake, cookies and cream, Thai tea, salted caramel, and grasshopper [1][2][7][9]. The toppings selection includes sprinkles, crushed Oreos, crushed/chopped nuts, crushed candy bars, cherries, strawberries, chocolate chips, gummies, M&Ms, gummy worms, coconut, bananas, and pineapple [1]. However, the availability of specific flavors like cookies and cream can be inconsistent [2][8], and toppings options may not always be consistent [8].

[1] This place is so shockingly dirty and run down it amazes me they are not closed by the health department. They have not repaired so many broken tiles and other surfaces, exposing difficult to clean/porous surfaces, that I am sure roaches must run rampant at night. The staff allows the trash to pile up and spill out of the receptacles and the floor under the mats the cooks walk on looks like it is never cleaned. Soda station also looks hard to thoroughly clean and likely a perfect spot for bugs. Absolutely gross. That said, great fries. I tell myself I am building up my immune system when I gather the guts up to grab an order and get out before I psych myself out to the point I can't eat them.Bottom line, they seem to be busy enough so why the owner doesn't show his/her clients enough respect to remodel or at least provide a clean setting is disrespectful in my opinion.
[2] Usually I'm very happy with my visits but today i checked online to compare pricing for various restaurants and found Angelo's to be the best.  Imagine my surprise when i walked in to find them not only higher then posted online, but they absolutely refused to match the prices on their own menu, but they also refused to give me the contract info for their absentee boss.  I was told that he would be in around lunch time when i was trying to get breakfast for my family.  How does lunch time help me get breakfast?I have been a customer of this location for over 10yrs and don't think I'll ever be back.  How difficult is it to match the price and make sure it gets changed?  If you want to post your prices online it is your responsibility to update them.  If you don't then you should be willing to honor the posted pricing.  Extremely Bad Service!!! Very unhappy.
[3] You know what you will get at any of the three locations in Oceanside.
[4] I ordered the kids meal and they charged me for a large meal. I didn't complain about that though. After I had two chicken tenders and a small amount of fries I felt sick. I couldn't even make it  before everything came back up. I ended up with food poisoning.
[5] We've now been to every location and they are all great! Breakfast lunch or dinner, I can always find something yummy here. Highly recommend the pastrami burger.
[6] Feels like a delicious hole in the wall type of place but there's like 3 of them on this street. The menu is huge but everything we got was on point. Good work!
[7] The customer service was bad and the dude at the drive through was probably having a bad day
[8] What's the thing about Angelo's. It's different. It's unique and it's only in oceanside. They sport the red and yellow. Which at first I thought it was an In N out. But the Hispanic version! There menu is creative and diverse. From gyros, to burgers and burritos. Breakfast all day. Unique desert menu. Your eyes are glued to the big sign in the drive through trying to decide what to pick. The price maybe be a little. But the portions sizes. You will get it for lunch and dinner. You will see these locations scattered as if it was a field of flowers. Don't be fooled. They good. I call it one of Oceanside's unique talents. Whether you dine in our hit the drive through. It's good. The staff has your back when you order extra ranch, unlike other places that charge you a nickle, dime, and if they super stingy. A quarter. Have your wallet full and a deep appetite. Try this place out!
[9] It's always a hit or miss at Angelo's. Sometimes it's awesome, sometimes its not.
[10] Although it takes a little while to get your food, it's always worth it! The staff is friendly and the food is delicious. My family and I love to order the California burritos here. The carne asada they cook for it, is amazing!

Real Customer Reviews (Total: 10):
[1] Been here twice and will definitely come again, great burgers.  Their pretzel bun is a great compliment to a great burger.  I went simple cheese 2/3 lb patty and regular fixin's.  BUT this is not to convince you of what this place is really about...poutine!  The pulled pork poutine is to die for.  If you want a simple plain ol' Poutine you'll be pleasantly surprised.  Good curd and good gravy and tons of options to please all the foodies out there.  Come give it a try you won't be disappointed.
[2] Just had my first bite of porky's and all I can do is cuss on how good it is... Defiantly eating here again
[3] This place serves the best poutine I've ever had.  Oh wait this was the first time I had them so that's not a fair review.  Nonetheless, man it's good.  The cheese curds are from a special place in the midwest I believe.  I've been here at least three times ordering something different each time and have not been disappointed.  They're very nice people that own and operate the place.  They work hard and still smile at all their customers.
[4] Overhyped.  Good service but the food itself is a little bland and not interesting or anything to revisit.
[5] Have not been in months due to improperly cooked food that resulted in stomach pain.  I notified young man working there by phone and he could care less.  I thought I would give them another chance. Today.  Me and my son both got sick immediately after eating a couple of French fries in the poutin.  Stomach pain and vomiting.  Attempted to call . No answer.  I’m done with this place.  Another 20 dollars for one burger and  Poutin.  Any reputable place would be very concerned about food that makes customers sick.
[6] We live about 25 miles away, but often make the trek to eat here. There are a lot of other places that make something they claim is "Poutine", but somehow they screw up the gravy or cheese.  Not here. The Authentic is the best poutine I've had outside of Quebec! Crispy fries, squeaky cheddar cheese curds, amazing Brown gravy. Mmmmm.The Spuddburger is basically an Authentic on a burger... possibly the best of both Canadian and American comfort food.  So good!The staff and owner are friendly and helpful. Spudds is definitely recommended, especially if you're a Canadian expat looking for a real taste of home.
[7] Came here to try the poutine a few months back and was a pleasant treat. Saw the menu and had to come back to give it full juatice, I came here with a friend and had ordered the Slawburger, Crispy chicken burger with a side of friend cheese curds and fries. Food was great just not a fan of brioche buns but they have a pretzel bun alternative overall amazing staff great food and environment! Definitely a place to try out and come back too.
[8] The poutine tasted very good, but needed more cheese as we do it in Canada.
[9] 2.5 to 3*** Poutine good but not enough cheese curds or gravy. Burger was fresh, meat was good but guacamole tasted so-so and looked like it was purchased and not made from scratch. I could be wrong.  Brioche bun was nice.  Cheese selections for burgers very limited- american or swiss only.  Prices are a bit high for the ingredients offered. Drink selection is very limited too. Parking is tight as there are other popular stores in the corner mall. Sign is small so be on the lookout.  Seating is limited.
[10] Authentic poutine as far as I can tell. Good burgers as well. Tiny parking lot at a pretty busy intersection.

User Question: What are the standout menu items at Spudds, and what do customers say about the quality of the poutine?

Customers highlight the poutine and burgers as standout menu items at Spudds. The poutine is praised for its authentic taste, with specific mentions of crispy fries, squeaky cheddar cheese curds, and amazing brown gravy, making it the best some have had outside of Quebec [6]. Options like the pulled pork poutine are described as 'to die for' [1], and the Authentic poutine is noted for good curd and gravy [1]. However, some reviews mention that the poutine could use more cheese curds or gravy [8][9]. Burgers, such as the Spuddburger and those with pretzel buns, are also highly recommended, with the Spuddburger combining poutine elements on a burger [6] and the pretzel bun complementing the burger well [1]. Other items like the Slawburger and crispy chicken burger are enjoyed, though some note limited cheese selections for burgers [7][9].

Then they went back to talking. When it was seated at the table with my friends a man who look like he could be a server or waiter waltzed in with an air of authority threw a stack of napkins down on the table and walked around looking us over did not announce himself great us welcome us or anything. I looked at the choices of soup I did not want to eat meat there was a choice of chicken broth with different choices. I chose the chicken broth with the vegetables. The server stated the only vegetable that they could put in there was carrots. I said all right that would be fine. When my soup arrived it was lukewarm in temperature. The Taste was closer to slightly salted tap water really no flavor at all. And there were beautiful carrots floating in the lukewarm water. I told the server that did not want the soup as there was no flavor to it and it wasn't hot.  The soup was taken away immediately and I was not charged. So we waited longer to get the right pizza. Because we had to wait (45 mins) we were given the Hawaiin pizza and an item from their bakery. The food and pastries ( we bought some sweets too) was delish! Only downfall was the wait time - regardless of the wrong pizza being made. Call first and order, check it before leaving & you'll  be more than satisfied. You'll  be Very happy with their food. A bit pricey but TOTALLY worth the $.

Being a native New York, I of course noticed the word "Manhattan" right away and was intrigued by the prospects of "Manhattan in the Desert".  Stopped in with family for lunch and was not disappointed!  The decor is upscale casual with very comfortable booths.  Large black and white vintage prints of NY scenes hang on the walls.  The menu is huge! Took 10 minutes just to make a decision!  Decided to go with a classic Ham, Salami and Swiss sandwich on rye with side of potato salad. Terrific place with friendly staff as was the manager, Itai was very friendly and grateful for our business.  Will be sure to visit again next time I'm in the desert!

The food was good with large portions. Plenty of parking. On a Saturday night, at 6:30 pm, in the middle of 113° heat, the place wasn't completely full but almost.I had clipped a $11.99 summer dinner coupon that motivated me to come by. However, it was part of their normal menu, but no big deal. I'm sure they will continue for the remainder of the summer season.Large and varied menu that should handle most people.I ordered the turkey dinner which came with soup or salad, roast turkey slices, and two vegetable sides for $11.99. Drink was extra. As I said earlier, the portions were very generous and I left stuffed. However, I couldn't resist getting a Key Lime Bar to go. With tax and tip, the total was under thirty.A couple of weeks ago I went to Sherman's, which is surprisingly similar in every way. However, of the two, the food and service at Manhattan was much better. Cost was the same for their dinner special.With Manhattan's other restaurant in Palm Desert closing recently, for which I hadn't heard anything good about, I was hesitant to come here. But not now as I look forward to coming back soon.Definitely a place I would recommend.

If I did not hear the order filler say the wrong side, I would have been disappointed. I would eat here again.

[1] I would recommend ACE AUTOMOTIVE to anyone. Great service , nice people and very reasonable prices. Honest people.
[2] I've been with Ace for over 12 years now. I refer all my friends and family. Shy, the new owner, has really been a great fit for the shop and would recommend anyone looking for a great and honest mechanic!
[3] Best mechanic in town... okay, in the state. Love Shy (and miss Robert)

[5] Going to a trustworthy mechanic is as common as finding  a needle in a hey stack, finding SOMEBODY THAT that will shoot you straight on things is pretty rare i am a 23 yr old male that is pretty handy with most things i am  no mechanic but can fix the minor problems and i like to but against my own pride and ego and dealing with a busy schedule i took my truck in to the only mechanic my family and i trust they are  fixing my truck  as we speak but i cant  tell you how much of an awesome experience it is. My message is for anyone that is mechanically savvy or not, save yourself some time and bring it in to ace. Shy will always be more honest with you than anyone you know and shoot you great prices i am not someone to write reviews or beat my chest about service but this is just one of my simple ways of them thanking them for saving me time and stress its been a pleasure
[6] IN SHORT:I am VERY disappointed with ACE Automotive. Don't go there, especially for an estimate. They will charge you for doing nothing! I wanted to give them 2 stars because I think they had a good manner about them and were polite, if dismissive of what I thought might be wrong, but charging a customer for doing nothing is VERY POOR CUSTOMER SERVICE. It seems that the only month it might be safe to go there is July, when AAA comes by to give them a certificate assuring they have great customer service.FULL STORY:I was referred to Ace by Kiwi Motors down the street who did excellent work on my wife's Honda. Went in to Ace because my car was running a little rough and there was an oil leak and some door handles that needed to be replaced. I liked the guys when I first went in, they assured me that they would give the car a thorough look over and let me know what needed to be done. I had the car in by 8:00AM today to give them the maximum time to look it over. I let them know that I didn't have much money, only a few hundred to do repairs for an oil leak and maybe a few other things if they were affordable. I brought the new door handles I ordered online in with me. By 10:30, they called me back. I won't name names, but the person on the other end of the phone assured me that he had looked it over for half an hour: The rough idle was from old engine mounts. Replacing the door handles using the parts I had brought to them would cost me $175. He couldn't check on the oil leak because he would have to take the covers off to find out where it was and that would cost a lot in labor, but the operation was likely to involve a repair that would cost over $500. That was only to fix the one possible oil leak and timing belt, because it was bound to break and strand me if I didn't take care of it (My wife had a new timing belt put on her car at Kiwi for about $250). There would be an additional $150+ charge to do a valve-cover gasket replacement (A simple DIY operation, less than $50 for parts, oil and oil pan at any automotive supplier and about 30-45 minutes to do yourself). Then he told me that there was a problem with the BRAND NEW alternator that I had installed less than six months ago. He suggested that I needed an entirely new alternator, which would be over $175 for them to do with labor and parts, because there was a spark gap in the lug holding the lead into the alternator and he didn't want to touch it because it was hot. I immediately went to get my car, this sounded fishy...I was charged $45 to get my key back for nothing besides some advice (I don't know what is laborious about driving my car into the port and looking under the hood for 20 minutes, probably less). I drove it home, replaced the washer and lug-nut holding the lead on the alternator. The lead was clean; there was no melted metal and no corrosion or whitening/blackening from sparking. Took me LESS than one minute to do everything. No more alternator light. Nothing wrong with alternator. Took the door panels off and replaced the door handles, replaced door panels. Less than 30 minutes to do both handles myself!I called another local place (Big John's, THANK YOU) and asked them what they would charge for an estimate and a thorough look over of the car: Nothing. Estimates and inspections done for free. The guy even gave me advice on how to do some of the work myself. THAT is great customer service.
[7] Stopped by for a diagnostic after work , not knowing if I would have to wait , I didn’t have an appointment, But nope !!! My car was taken to the work area ASAP, maybe about 30 minutes or so I had some feed back about my car . Super easy to work with the owner and his coworker Steve, very nice & felt they were both honest with what they think about my car.  I will be going back when I’m ready to drop a new transmission in my car .
[8] I was travelling to Los Angeles and my car broke down in Valencia.  I was towed to Ace Automotive thinking the worst and I remember Evan, the tow truck driver saying what a great mechanic Shy was over there and that he'd get me back on the road in no time, plus it was a Friday.  Evan wasn't lying!  Shy popped the hood and fiddled for like three minutes and knew exactly what the problem was.  He was able to fix it and get me back on the road under an hour.  He was kind, courteous, knowledgeable and quick!  And he was honest!  He easily could've taken advantage of me and my wallet as I was a stranger in town but he didn't.  The whole repair was under $100 and I was happy to pay him!  My car made it to its destination and all the way back home.  Shy even called me on Monday to make sure I returned safely and if everything was OK.  If live in Valencia or are ever stranded there by fate, do not hesitate to have your car towed to ACE AUTOMOTIVE!  I wish they were located here in Fresno as Shy would be my permanent mechanic for life!  Shy if you're reading this, thank you again and thank Evan the tow guy too - he was also honest with me and wise enough to tow me to you.  Wishing you and the business many successes.  ;)

[10] This is a Great repair shop. Such honest, caring guys. I have referred all my family and friends. My Mom loves Robert!  Who wouldn't!  He explains everything in terms you can understand.  I feel lucky to have found these guys.

[1] Being a major fan of the Senior Grubby's in the village of Carlsbad, I was enthralled to learn they had just opened a new location in downtown Oceanside just blocks from my house.Same great food and service in a new fun and relaxed environment.Awesome location just blocks from the beach with lots of space and outdoor seating. I ordered a shrimp fajita and a Razzle-Dazzle burrito. Both amazing! I could tell the shrimp was fresh as well as all the veggies. Must visit!!
[2] Super chill place, one of the best burritos I've had!
[3] Cowboy burrito and tortas are pretty good and a nice size but the tacos have no flavor and aren't worth it at all
[4] I ate at this restaurant last night and was thoroughly disappointed. The cashier taking the order was nice enough but, for a “Taco Tuesday” this place was grossly understaffed. I’m visiting from AZ and find the fish tacos in the middle of the desert to be more satisfactory. The food was unflavored and a staff member literally sat down at a table next to us to chat with what I assumed were regulars while we went on eating without napkins or utensils. I’m usually not a stickler but I receive better service regularly at places that are less expensive. I don’t understand what the concept of this place is? Fast food, self serve or sit down?
[5] Great for breakfast,  brunch and dinner,  great place for some football!
[6] Love eating at this place. Great atmosphere and food and service
[7] On a warm Saturday night in Oceanside, California, four college aged boys walked into Senór Grubby’s. Not expecting much these boys were in no way shape or form prepared for what was about to happen to them. As their life was about to change forever. When the burritos and nachos came to their table what happened next was nothing short of a miracle. What entered their mouths was probably the greatest thing to ever happen to things in all of human history. Half way through my Carlsbad burrito i realized I was fully erect and blood flow was beginning to not get to my brain. Luckily, the servers were on top of their game and gave each of the boys the equivalent to an olympic swimming pools worth of water to ensure our survival. After finishing the meal four men collected themselves and walked outside. Thank you grubby’s, we will never forget you.
[8] Will definitely return. Met a friend, laughed with the tender and all around good vibez
[9] Nice addition to the Downtown Oceanside area. Cool interior  Had the al pastor burrito which was pretty good. A little pricey for the size but probably expected for the area.
[10] Love this place for years and so does my family! Some of us are vegetarian which can be challenging finding a good menu that everyone will enjoy. Señor Grubby's cooks knows how to make a tasty veggie burrito! This is our top three options for easy dining. Service is quick and the food is often hot even after some travel. I only have one caution recently, the same day my card was charged here, there was a another charge at a gas station near $100. Not sure if my info was compromised a week or more earlier but I hadn't used the card for a week and the charge landed the same day within a few hours after food purchase. [1] David and his crew did an awesome job on our new roof. We love it! Due to my insurance company this project was a nightmare from the beginning. We greatly appreciated their efforts. Once approved, David and his crew went to work. They were all respectful, on time, and communicated with us during the entire process. Would recommend.
[3] CBX replaced our roof due to hail damage. Ray was the initial contact who met with our adjuster. Colton directed the team that completed the work in a couple of days.  Both were easy to work with and very committed to our satisfaction.  They suggested a couple of  upgrades and our roof looks amazing.
[4] Robert and his crew were top notch, worked hard to get the job complete and left my property clean when they were done. The new roof looks great.
[5] We had CBX replace our roof after a hail storm here in town. They were very responsive at the beginning when they were trying to acquire our business. After we signed the contract it all changed. I had called several times to get the schedule date and did not have anyone call me back after several calls. When I finally heard from them and received a date, we scheduled a few days before I left for vacation so that I could be home for the project. I had a large dog that would not have behaved well with them working and I wanted to be there to keep the dog from barking etc. They call me a couple of days before the start date and they had to push the project back. I wasn't happy but I understood. They completed the project within a few days. It took several weeks for the final inspection to come back. When they finally charged me it was much more than the original insurance approved amount. The original quote was $14,000 and the final bill was $21,000. My insurance ended up not covering all of it and money I was given for other hail damage repairs went to the roof bill. I was trying to work out the details with my insurance company to see if they would cover more as I was having to pay more than my deductible. Suddenly I heard from CBX all the time requesting payment. When I needed to get ahold of someone I never heard anything but when money was due they were contacting me often. I had a small portion of the final bill that was due after insurance basically told me I should have shopped around for pricing. I sent an email to the bill employee after I made a partial payment that I would pay the remaining balance in a few weeks. She said that was great. About a week later I get a notice in the mail that a lien was placed on my house. No warning at all. I could have found a way to pay this had I known this was going to be happening. My parents who had 2 roofs done by them received a lien as well around the same time frame. It understand the need to collect payment but I feel the customer service was lacking the entire time. A little notice would have been great. I highly recommend anyone looking to get a roof done by cbx to weigh other options. I know other people that had good quality roofs done with less headaches for much cheaper.
[6] So, really happy with CBX roofs and their team on the ground, they have done a great job with our roof replacement due to hail damage, and even negotiated with insurance to get our outbuildings included, which was not part of the original claim. Great leadership from Robert, and just an efficient and helpful company. I highly recommend them.
[7] I recommend CBX Roofing to everyone in and around Redding. When David from CBX arrived at my house in early December we were supposed to have our roof completed within a few days. But then a series of unfortunate and unforeseen events ensued that kept the project from being completed until mid-January. So how does CBX rate so highly on a project that took longer than originally expected? I could go on and on about how great our experience with CBX was, but I will keep it to three main reasons.First, when the old roof was removed they found a dangerous configuration of illegal wiring that had been installed many years ago by the previous homeowner. CBX worked with the city on our behalf to ensure that the future design was up to code and, more importantly, safe for my family. They also coordinated with our insurance company to get an electrician to bid the project as quickly as possible. But then the rains came. For those of you in this part of the country, you understand our rainy season is quite unpredictable but pretty consistently wet through December and January. We knew we would be roofless for much longer than we wanted. We were nervous, but this was the second time that CBX rose to the occasion. David and team sprang into action and made sure our roof was tarped to protect us from the crazy wind and rain we experienced over the course of 3-4 weeks. During that time we had a couple of leaks (water always finds a way in), but each time it happened David was onsite to readjust or add a layer of tarp to prevent any actual damage, one time even at 6:45 on a Sunday morning. But the CBX team didn’t stop there. The third house-saving/money-saving/life-saving/marriage-saving moment came when the CBX office went to bat for us with our insurance claim. They fought for us and ended up saving us thousands of dollars because of their understanding of claims, codes, and the overall insurance process.This could have been a nightmare experience, but with CBX it was merely a little bump in the road. The entire team we interacted with: Dan, Bob, Ron, McNeal, and Jeremy to name a few… everyone involved was friendly, professional, hard-working, and helpful. If you want someone to make sure that your roof not only gets done right, but also that you will have an excellent experience from start to finish, then call CBX. I am sure all of the Project Managers are great there, too, but if I were you I would specifically ask for David. For us he was the difference. He kept us informed, he was always accessible (sorry for all the calls and texts, David), and he even made sure my children kept their distance from any nails on the ground (which his team soon picked once they got off the roof!).

[9] The crew was very professional. They were kind and honest. They cleaned up after they were done. We had to have the old roof removed and a new one put on. When they left it was very clean and we were very happy. Easy to communicate with. I would recommend them to anyone.

Appt. 10:30 no show...called office at 11:00 gave them my name, #, address - 3 times....she said she would send someone out...I said I had an appointment at 1pm so needed someone fairly quickly.  Someone called 1 hour later - said he got the wrong address and he was on his way.  I said why didn't you call me - didn't have my number - why didn't you call the office an hour and 1/2 ago for my phone # and address.Then he repeated my address wrongly...I said I didn't have a good feeling about this...I used to be a sale rep and I really wouldn't handle this way.  He said haven't you ever gotten the wrong information from your office -yes but I called right away...He waited 1 1/2 hour to call me.  I asked if he had the samples I asked for - I was interested in lighter color one...all he had was darker color....You know this really wasn't going well.  I thought I would give them a chance and ask to speak to a manager - to get someone more experienced out to my home...at this point I really didn't trust what this person would write for an estimate.  I called the office asked for a manager - why - I'm a disgruntled maybe soon to be customer..we don't have any managers - just us in the office.  Hold on I may have someone that can' help you...is he a manager...pause, yes.Are you a manager...pause...yes...whats your name , gives me first name, whats your last name.  I need to know who I'm talking to before I give you my last name.  Well, for a manager not to give me his name, not a manager.  IF I HAD THIS MUCH TROUBLE TRYING TO GET TO A MANAGER, HOW DO YOU THINK I WOULD DO IN A YEAR, AFTER I HAVE PAID THEM FOR MY ROOF.......BIG LESSON...I WILL BUY LOCAL!!!

[6] I just began playing hockey and bought my skates and most of my gear from monkeysports. The prices are competitive to the market and seem fair. The sales staff is very friendly and enthusiastic about getting you all set. But if you don’t get their attention right away you may find yourself waiting 30+ minutes for help. I’ve have a couple recent negative experiences explaining the 2-star review. First, they said they’d put on a clear face mask to my helmet, which they did but put it on upside down. Fortunately someone at the rink had the tools I needed to redo it properly. Second, the sales staff said my skates were good to go upon leaving the store. After my first game I found out they never sharpened the skates.  I’m new to the sport and I’m inexperienced, so I trusted what they said and that they knew what they were doing. After learning the hard way (I’ve got the bruises to prove it), I’ve been able to see what they failed to tell me and what I need to keep an eye on for my equipment from here on out. It’s still the closest hockey retailer to me currently but I would just do my research on every last thing you need before you get on the ice rather than just go by what they’re saying.
[7] Everyone employed there are always helpful. The service and knowledge are at a best. Mason helped my younger son this time with hockey gear. I give him props!!!

[9] Disappointing interaction with this store.  I bought a new USA Baseball Standard  ~$100 bat at full price for my son's upcoming season. I called the manufacturer and they did say that their policy was to handle warranty issues directly through them and not the store.  But, in light of the equipment failure such a short time after purchase, they encouraged me to go back to the store to try to get a direct exchange so my son would not be without a bat for ~2 weeks mid season.  The store manager showed no interest or flexibility in considering our request.  They are willing to sell you the bat at full price but do not stand by the product.  We had better service with an Easton bat quality concern through the now out of business Sports Authority.  So be aware that you may be out the cost of the bat and have no functioning piece of equipment while dealing with the manufacturer and shipping items back and forth through the mail.
[10] I went to Monkey Sports to buy new ice skates. The salesman who helped me was very friendly and accommodating. I tried on several pairs of skates before settling on the Vapor X7.0. I asked the salesman if Monkey Sports offered free skate sharpening for the life of the skates if I bought the skates there (Hockey Giant offers this service).  He said yes, so I got the skates baked, I paid and left.I came back about a week later to get the skates sharpened and was asked to pay $6 at the counter. I questioned the cashier's reason for charging me and explained that the salesman promised me free skate sharpening. He returned a few minutes later only to confirm what the cashier had told me: basically, "sorry dude.  Buyer beware." I left the store quite frustrated.About a month after my purchase, I wrote a polite letter to Monkey Sports management explaining what happened.  After a few weeks of no response, I sent an email to the address on their website.  They never replied to the letter or the email.I have since replaced the Vapors with Supreme NXGs purchased at Hockey Giant.  The skates even came with a little HG sticker on the bottom of the boot that gives me free skate sharpening at Giant for the life of the skates.Monkey Sports, you have made me a Hockey Giant customer for life.
