description: This Japanese place offers traditional Japanese barbecue & other dishes, a sushi bar & private wooden booths.
address: Robataya Oton, 5447 Kearny Villa Rd, San Diego, CA 92123

messages.content: You are a professional local business analyst. Please objectively and accurately answer user questions about a specific business based on the provided context. Be honest if you don't know.

Business Name: Robataya Oton
Category: Authentic Japanese restaurant, Japanese restaurant
Description: This Japanese place offers traditional Japanese barbecue & other dishes, a sushi bar & private wooden booths.

Real Customer Reviews (Total: 10):
[1] I was so very happy to see that this place was open again for dine-in that I had to do a review!  Hands down my favorite place for authentic Japanese cuisine.  It has the heart and soul of home cooking but yet subtly elevated.  It is fine dining for sure without being pretentious.  It has a cute and humble interior, with no fancy bells n whistles but the food definitely shines and speaks for itself!  If you are not familiar with Japanese cuisine, I recommend going in with an open mind and just be adventurous; this is not the kinda place to get a California roll.  If you don't like sushi before you don't like to eat raw fish then great news...so much stuff on the menu is cooked and to perfection!  Speaking of cooking...the shabu shabu is the best I've ever had!  You have to order at least 2 orders of the beef shabu shabu but it's a lot of food and totally worth it!  All the other dishes are also very yummy and perfectly seasoned.  I've ordered almost everything on the menu and there's not one thing I didn't like.  The staff is also great and very friendly.  We always get the same server and she's been so amazing!  I do recommend making reservations as it can get pretty busy for dinner service.  Each booth is its own little private room so it's perfect for social distancing during this pandemic.  Great food takes time to prepare.  Some dishes come out quickly but and some might take a bit longer.  I recommend having some sake while you wait.  I promise you it is well worth the wait!  I can't wait to come back soon!
[2] Japanese food and service here is very good. I love eating here. The staff was very helpful and welcoming. I can see why they have so many great reviews.
[3] Great little Japanese restaurant, very authentic, it's small but cozy. There's a bar area and a traditional small dining rooms. The food is amazing, there are many small dishes and some mains for individuals. We chose to order many small dishes and every dish was flavorful and so delicious.  The service was amazing, polite, helpful and responsive.  The only small complaint was since the restaurant is so small, it was packed with supplies and I can see shoes from all the diners in the rooms. Luckily they didn't stink but a slight eye sore. However the excellent food and service out weighs the eye sore as I was facing the chef and kitchen
[4] Awesome sushi omakase from chef Takumi, beautiful atmosphere and super friendly staff! I'll definitely go back next time I visit San Diego
[5] Small, unassuming location, buzzing but not too loud, attentive service and friendly staff, tasty food portioned nicely with good variety at fair prices. Would go back! Make a reservation before you go.
[6] Ambience: 4 stars Food:  2 stars value:  1 star  I don't normally write reviews, but I feel so jaded and ripped off, that I have to write.  This place was highly recommended by a friend of ours.  Our friends are foodies, so I trusted their judgement.  Before you enter the establishment, you must first find parking.  Parking is incredibly hard to find, and you have to spend a good portion of your time driving around.  Once you enter, you are greeted by very friendly hostesses who guide you to your booth.  The place is in traditional Japanese decor and layout, something you would see on Anthony Bourdain.  Its very nice and novel if you haven't experienced anything like this before.  However, the pillows are worn, the decor is old and drab.  The wood is fading.  The place generally needs an update.  My friend and I, there was only two of us, ordered the traditional HotPot and a sake.  Our bill came out to be a little over $80.  That is $40 each person for a bowl of soup and a couple glasses of Sake.  This is extremely expensive and in my opinion not worth it.  First, the menu is confusing and difficult to follow.  Its poorly laid out and hard to figure out what you want.  So we asked for advice and went with the traditional dish.  So, what do we get for all this money?  Did we get better service?  Better food?  Incredible food?  Little details that make you go "wow"?  None of the above.  The soup consisted of $2 worth of mushrooms, 50 cents of cabbage, 50 cents of noodles, 50 cents worth of some green herb, some green onions and lets say $10 worth of beef, maybe half a pound.  I'm erroring on their side. A couple eggs, and very salty broth.    That comes out to maybe $13.50-$15.  Was there any extraordinary preparation involved like a souffle?  No.  Everything is raw, thrown in a pot and let to cook on your table.  So what am I paying for?  We did not receive glasses of water, did not receive hot tea.  The service wasn't any better then any where else.  I love to eat out, I've been to many hot pot places and foreign restaurants, but this was the first I felt no way was it worth it.  I had to apologize to my friend profusely for the exuberance of the night.  So its your choice.  If you decide to go, at least you know more than you did before.  The number of 5 stars on this place is misleading, very misleading.
[7] Our food took almost an hour to come out! We ordered a main and some side dishes. Beer was fast, side dishes came in little by little but main dish arrived at 9pm, we were seated at 8:15 (reservation was at 8)
[8] Best Japanese food I've had outside of Japan. A bit pricy, but you get traditional atmosphere with top rate food.
[9] The sashimi is very fresh. The serving is small, pricey but worth it for the quality
[10] Fantastic, truly authentic Japanese Izakaya style food and environment. I highly recommend this place to any and all Japanese food enthusiasts. ごちそうさまでした！！！！！

User Question: What are some of the standout dishes at Robataya Oton, and how is the overall dining experience described by customers?

Customers highlight several standout dishes, including the shabu shabu, which is described as the best ever and worth ordering at least two servings [1], and the sashimi, noted for being very fresh [9]. The sushi omakase from chef Takumi is also praised as awesome [4]. The overall dining experience is described as authentic Japanese, with a cozy and traditional atmosphere featuring private wooden booths and a sushi bar [1][3][10]. Service is generally reported as great, friendly, and attentive [1][2][3][5], though one review mentioned slow service with a main dish taking nearly an hour to arrive [7]. The food is often considered amazing, flavorful, and perfectly seasoned [1][3], but some customers find it pricey [6][8][9], with one negative review criticizing the value and decor as needing an update [6]. Reservations are recommended due to busy dinner service [1][5].

When I arrived in Florida I received an email stating that the package had been delivered. My first thoughts were great! Shortly thereafter, I found out that it had been delivered to my address in Atlanta not the address I had given Aaron the previous day.  Obviously, I was unable to use the equipment I had ordered and I am sure they would take it back if I want to go through the effort of shipping it to them. However, the real point here is that they had four opportunities to get this right but somehow found a way to screw it up. Each time I asked what happened they blamed "the system".  It is their "system" and someone might want to take a look at it.
[10] Arthur, I sent you a separate email.  Thanks.Shared this on Yelp as well.First, I want to start by saying I do not like giving negative feedback.  I think everyone has had bad days!  But I would expect a company to keep their promises and commitments and if they can't that they come up with a creative solution.I have never had a problem with scuba.com when it was owned by the former owner, and I have never had problems with their, now, sister company Leisurepro.  They are now both owned by Adorama.  I have spent literally over $4,000 over the last 8 months with scuba.com and it is my most recent purchase that has caused me to write this review.  I share the amount I have spent, no to brag, but to put into context that I did a lot of business with scuba.comMy last (and final) purchase from scuba.com was a steel HP80 that had to be shipped from their sister store back in New Jersey.  This was right before the COVID-19 thing happened.  I was going to pick it up at the store but because the store was closed, I had directed UPS that I would pick up the tank directly from UPS's OC hub (UPS tried to deliver twice to their store but could not deliver it, so I was trying to be empathetic and not have UPS have to ship it back to Leisurepro and then for Leisurepro to accrue additional freight to ship it back to me).  This was in March.Part of the purchase of the tank was they would assemble the tank, do the VIP, and fill the tank.When I called their customer service at the beginning of the week (6/22), I was told that their new location in Costa Mesa would not be open till August.  Well, I would like to go diving and would love to use my new tank to do so, and really do not want to wait till August!  So I called again today (6/25) and wanted to know if I could take the tank to another LDS which was open to do the VIP, assembly, and air fill, and if scuba.com could credit the $15 to my account.The instructor first did not understand what I was talking about, and after some clarification on my end, he offered to put it together but that they could not fill the tank as their new location did not have a compressor.  I again asked if I could go and get the thing done by another LDS and have them credit my account.  Which the instructor just repeated what he told me before.  Also, he noted that the tank should have a "red shark" label on it indicating that the tank had VIP.  I told him that I had not even opened the box so did not know if it did have the label.  Well, after the call I did open the box and the tank did not have a Red Shark label on it, so I guess it did not have a VIP.I got frustrated and told him that he had lost my business for good and that I would not do any more business with scuba.com.I wish the company well, and I realize that my experience may not be the same experience others will go through.  But I thought I would share my experience.  Thanks for reading this lengthy review!!!

User Question: What are the experiences with customer service and staff at Scuba.com?

Customers have reported both positive and negative experiences with the customer service and staff at Scuba.com. Positive interactions include prompt and knowledgeable support from staff members such as Dave, Darrick, Arthur, Kevin, Bob, and Aaron, with specific instances of helpful advice on equipment like wetsuits and efficient service for air fills [1][2][3][4][5][6][7][8]. However, some customers have encountered issues with order fulfillment, shipping delays, and miscommunication, particularly in cases involving inventory problems and delivery errors [9][10]. For example, one customer experienced multiple shipping mistakes and blamed the company's system for the failures [9], while another had difficulties with tank assembly and VIP services during the COVID-19 pandemic, leading to frustration and loss of business [10].

Real Customer Reviews (Total: 10):
[1] The food is amazing and so are the owners. Wish we lived closer.
[2] I have to admit, this pho restaurant made me feel quite euphoric. The waitress was rather nice to me and commented on my nice trench coat. Like a true gentleman, I tipped m'fedora out of respect and she brought out a complimentary side of spring rolls. So the customer service was outstanding and deserves a 10/10. Now for the food, oh my it was sublime! The hot broth made a sweat trickle down my neckbeard and the steam made my 10 inch framed glasses fog up. Truly, truly, an outrageous experience. 10/10 would eat again.
[3] Their wonton/dumpling soup is awesome.  Dumplings are incredible and from scratch.
[4] Good pho, spring rolls did not have much herbs in them. Will come back again.
[5] I love this place! The owners are very nice and speak English well,although, you either love there food or you hate it.
[6] Was looking forward to a banh mi  could not believe that they hardly had any meat or veggies the meat that was in their was good but honestly hardly any  very very disappointed.  Pass
[7] I love having lunch here. The people are so nice, remembering our favorite dishes. Mine is lemongrass tofu, while my husband loves pho #C6.
[8] I've found myself with a serious craving for a nice big bowl of pho lately, so I decided to head down from Marysville to give this place a go.  Now, I'm a bit of a purist when it comes to pho.  I don't like anything fancy or new-fangled; just a traditional steaming bowl of well-made broth, simple rice noodles, a good helping of the appropriate cuts of beef and a plate of fresh herbs and bean sprouts to compliment them.  Hit all these marks, and you've won a permanent place in my heart.  Here's how "Pho Saigon's" house special fared on all of these points:The Broth- Honestly, the broth was disappointing.  It lacked the depth of spice that is the hallmark of carefully made, good pho broth.  Traditional spices such as star anise and roasted ginger were nearly imperceptible and the resulting liquid was more akin to warm, beefy saltwater.  By no means was it bad or unpalatable, merely disappointing when you've had the truly good stuff before.The Noodles- Let's face it, it's pretty darn hard to mess up rice noodles.  Save for when they're severely undercooked and still brittle or overcooked and mushy, rice noodles are pretty generically rice noodles.  These were cooked appropriately and, of all the factors I look for in good pho, the only one I have no complaints about.The Meat- My favorite part of pho is by far and wide the meat.  Thinly sliced cuts of rare beef flank, Vietnamese-style meatballs and an assortment of cuts that are usually seen as undesirable in American cuisine (tripe, tendons and other fatty or cartilaginous bits and pieces) become pure heaven when slowly simmered in a good broth.  The meat at "Pho Saigon" was actually quite good, but the quantity was a major disappointment.  I requested (for an additional $2) extra meat to be put in my soup, but what I was given hardly amounted to what I would receive at my usual pho joints without requesting extra.  This was extremely obvious when I realized that when it came to the Vietnamese meatballs, I had only been given half of one.  Skimp on the noodles if you must, but go cheap on the meat and you've lost me.The Herb Plate- Pretty much standard to nearly all pho restaurants is the plate of herbs and bean sprouts that accompany your food.  "Pho Saigon" was no different.  This one consisted of a bed of fresh bean sprouts, two slices of raw jalapeno, a branch of none-to-fresh Thai basil that had been harvested far too late and half of a lemon wedge.  Again, they seemed to skimp in one of the key aspects of pho.  Aside from the overly mature basil being more bitter than fragrant, I had expected to have a choice between lemon and lime (and certainly more than half a wedge).For all of their shortcomings in terms of food quality, the waitress was extremely courteous and provided very good service throughout the entire meal.  The decor was simple and neat and provided a fairly uninteresting but pleasant eating environment.  All in all, "Pho Saigon" was worth the $10 for a large-sized bowl (though I have had better for less), but not the 45-minute drive.  I may or may not return to this restaurant in the future.  It's one of those take it or leave it situations.
[9] Have been there twice now with friends from church...lots to choose from. E-44 is yummy!!
[10] Have you ever had one of those moments when you taste or smell something, and it instantly takes you back to a moment in your past? My dinner tonight did just that. I ordered the grilled pork and egg roll over vermicelli (Bun). It took my back to a  Ho Chi Mihn City, Vietnam, sitting in a small, street side restaurant with my friend. The flavor was spot on to the Bun I ordered that night. Probably the most authentic flavor I’ve had since being there back in 2016. Glad I found this place. My only complaint is that the portion was a little small for the price. But, if you’re looking for authentic Vietnamese flavor, definitely eat here. You won’t be disappointed.

User Question: What are the customer opinions on the portion sizes and value for money at Pho Saigon?

Customer reviews indicate mixed opinions on portion sizes and value for money at Pho Saigon. Some customers found the portions to be small for the price, such as in the grilled pork and egg roll over vermicelli dish [10] and the pho, where extra meat was requested but the quantity was disappointing [8]. However, other reviews mention that the food was worth the price, with one customer stating it was worth $10 for a large bowl of pho, though they had better for less [8]. There are no reviews explicitly praising large portions or exceptional value, but some customers plan to return despite criticisms [4][10].

[1] They took care of my 2001 Sienna in the past when other shops could not find the problem. Just got my axel replaced and once again job well done. Excellent service.Thank you Jason and teamDuane Johnson

I identified that I had an oil leak and was losing a substantial amount of fluid in a short amount of time and my idle fluctuating between 600-800RPM; not constant. The job required the front end of the car to be disassembled to locate and isolate the leak(s). They identified that the oil cooler gasket and oil filter stand gasket were the cause of my problems. They called me to inform me of the problem and solution prior to conducting the work. Through the process, e-mailed pictures of the location of the leak and the disassembly. They checked all my fluids while conducting the repairs, and informed me that the car was well over due for transmission fluid (provided photos as well). I recently bought the car used with 68k miles, and wouldn't be surprised if it was the original fluid, so I had them change it as well. I also needed new air mass meter boot, the original was held on by the hose clamp tied to a metal hose by a wire. With the amount of work to be done, my car had to be left over night to finish up the work the next day (Tuesday). Jason called me mid morning to tell me everything was put back together and that the engine makes a terrible 'hiss'. Being the boot had been faulty, and likely leaking--not creating a sufficient seal the hiss wasn't noticeable. The PVC valve's diaphragm was faulty and on the verge of failing which would have created a vacuum within the engine and displaced the engine oil. I authorized to have the valve cover replaced (PVC is embedded within). I picked up my Cooper Tuesday evening. She sounded great. I no longer had an idle problem. She ran great. I am a happy customer. I HIGHLY recommend them. I will be a return customer!! Thanks again guys!

The procedure was performed at Pearl Dental Care office by oral surgeon Dr. Tarun Patel. Both surgeries were performed flawlessly and without any postoperative complications. After complete Bone Graft Recovery I received 4 dental implants in the upper jaw. After implants successfully integrated with the surrounding bone the dental implants were completed and new partial dentures fitted very well.  Dr. Bhavin Changela demonstrated very high skill not only in general dentistry but also a high knowledge and experience in area of dental implants.  Pearl Dental Care is great dental office. Customer reviews indicate mixed experiences with service and sales at Merced Powersports. Positive aspects include friendly and helpful staff, professional service, and the potential for good deals or high selling prices [1][2][4][5][6][7][8][10]. However, some customers have reported issues such as low selling prices compared to expectations, difficulties in retrieving unsold items, and allegations of unethical practices like staged bidding or missing paperwork [3][9]. It is recommended to set a minimum price when selling to avoid disappointment [1].
